It aims to provide effective, results-driven personal training while avoiding the common pitfalls of the fitness industry. The company operates private gyms across four continents, including North America, Europe, Asia, the Middle East, and Australasia.

Specializing in elite personal training, Ultimate Performance uses a data-powered approach to create personalized training and nutrition plans. They track over 50 metrics in real-time through their proprietary UP Transform app, ensuring clients receive tailored support and accountability. The company employs top-tier personal trainers and emphasizes a holistic approach that includes physical training, nutrition, habits, and mindset.

Ultimate Performance operates state-of-the-art gyms in prestigious locations such as London and Los Angeles. With a diverse clientele, including celebrities, the company boasts a 97% success rate in helping clients achieve their fitness goals. Led by CEO Steve Brice, Ultimate Performance continues to expand its global presence and enhance its offerings.

A-Gas is a global leader in refrigerant lifecycle management, founded in 1993 in Bristol, UK. The company supports the HVACR (heating, ventilation, air conditioning, and refrigeration) industry, particularly during the transition away from chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) due to environmental regulations.

The Agri-Food and Biosciences Institute (AFBI) is a scientific organization located in Northern Ireland, employing around 650 staff. Established in 2006, AFBI focuses on high-technology research and development, as well as diagnostic and analytical testing. It operates as a non-departmental public body sponsored by the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA).

Apollo Fire Detectors Ltd is a UK-based company that designs and manufactures fire detection and alarm systems. Founded in 1980 and headquartered in Havant, Hampshire, the company operates globally, providing protection in over 100 countries. As part of the Halma Group, a leading life-saving technology conglomerate, Apollo is committed to quality and innovation in fire safety.

Bob W is a technology-driven hospitality company based in Helsinki, Finland, founded in 2018. It offers fully equipped short-stay apartments that serve as a smarter and more sustainable alternative to traditional hotels. By combining the best features of hotel experiences with the comfort of short-term rentals, Bob W delivers a four-star quality experience at a one-star cost.

Boohoo is a UK-based online fashion retailer founded in 2006, headquartered in Manchester. The company targets young consumers aged 16 to 30 with a fast-fashion model that offers trendy and affordable clothing and accessories. Boohoo operates exclusively online, which helps keep costs low and enables quick shipping. Most of its design and sourcing activities are based in the UK, particularly in cities like Leicester, London, and Manchester.

Brsk is a UK-based full-fibre broadband provider established in 2020 by Giorgio Iovino and Ian Kock. The company builds, owns, and operates its own fibre-optic network, delivering gigabit-capable internet to homes and businesses in underserved towns and suburban areas, including West Yorkshire, Lancashire, Greater Manchester, Cheshire, and the West Midlands.

ClientEarth is a global environmental non-profit organization dedicated to protecting life on Earth through legal advocacy and systemic change. Founded in 2007, it has grown to over 300 professionals, including lawyers and environmental specialists, with offices in major cities like London, Brussels, and Warsaw. The organization operates in more than 60 countries, combining local expertise with a global impact.

DICE (Dice FM Holdings Ltd) is a London-based ticketing software company founded in 2014. It focuses on providing a fan-first platform for discovering and purchasing tickets to live events, including concerts, festivals, and comedy shows. DICE aims to reshape the ticketing industry by creating a fairer and more inclusive experience for fans, artists, and venues.

Energy Aspects is a global provider of real-time market insights and data analytics focused on energy and related commodity markets. Founded in 2012 and headquartered in London, the company has offices in key locations including New York, Houston, Singapore, Dubai, Kolkata, Tokyo, and Athens, allowing for continuous market monitoring and client support.

The company specializes in comprehensive market intelligence that integrates physical market fundamentals, derivatives positioning, and data analytics across various sectors, including crude oil, natural gas, and agricultural commodities. Energy Aspects offers a client portal with expert-driven analytics, forecasts, and critical market analysis, along with tools for visualizing and accessing data from numerous sources. They also host events and webinars featuring industry leaders, fostering insights and networking opportunities.

With a diverse client base of over 600 organizations, including major energy companies and financial institutions, Energy Aspects provides actionable intelligence to support trading, investment, and strategic planning in the dynamic energy market. The company has expanded its expertise through acquisitions, enhancing its interconnected view of global markets.

English National Opera (ENO) is a prominent British opera company located in London, performing at the London Coliseum. It is one of the two main opera companies in the city, alongside The Royal Opera. ENO is unique for its commitment to presenting operas sung exclusively in English, which enhances accessibility for English-speaking audiences.

Founded in the late 19th century, ENO has a rich history that includes the establishment of the Sadler's Wells Opera Company in 1931. The company has a diverse repertoire, featuring works from early operas by Monteverdi to contemporary pieces and even Broadway shows. ENO is recognized for its innovative staging and high-quality musicianship, nurturing British talent and collaborating with esteemed conductors and directors.

In addition to live performances, ENO engages in educational outreach and community programs to broaden the appeal of opera. The company aims to attract a wide audience, including opera enthusiasts and local residents, by offering affordable ticket pricing. Overall, ENO plays a vital role in the cultural landscape of London, dedicated to making opera accessible and enjoyable for all.

Flagstone is a UK-based financial technology company that offers a high-interest cash deposit savings platform. It aims to help individuals, businesses, and charities manage, protect, and grow their cash savings efficiently. Users can access and open savings accounts from over 60 UK banks through a single application, benefiting from exclusive and market-leading interest rates. The platform is available to UK savers aged 18 and over, with a minimum deposit of £10,000, and also caters to international clients, including corporates, funds, and trusts.

Flagstone provides several key services, including savings account aggregation, which allows users to spread deposits across multiple accounts to maximize interest earnings. The platform also helps users maximize protection under the UK’s Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS), ensuring eligible cash deposits are protected up to £85,000 per client, per banking group. With features like a cash deposits calculator and easy online management, Flagstone simplifies the savings process while maintaining a focus on security and efficiency.

Foundry is a creative software company with nearly 30 years of experience, specializing in advanced software solutions for the Media and Entertainment industry. Based in London’s Soho district, the company has additional offices in the US, Canada, China, Japan, Australia, and Ireland. Foundry collaborates with major studios and post-production houses, including Industrial Light & Magic, DNEG, and Pixar.

The company offers a suite of industry-leading software tools such as Mari, Katana, Nuke, and Furnace. These tools have played a crucial role in the production of every VFX Academy Award-winning film over the past decade. Foundry is committed to advancing the art and technology of visual experience, providing powerful software for visual effects, animation, and post-production workflows. Its products are widely used by artists around the world to create high-quality digital content for entertainment media.

HackerOne is a cybersecurity company founded in 2012, based in San Francisco, CA. The company connects organizations with a global community of over 800,000 ethical hackers to identify and resolve security vulnerabilities. HackerOne operates primarily as a bug bounty platform, helping companies proactively discover security flaws before they can be exploited by malicious actors.

The company offers a range of services, including managing bug bounty programs, vulnerability coordination, and advisory support for efficient security issue remediation. HackerOne also provides an API for integration with other security tools, online training courses for security researchers, and hosts live hacking events to engage the security community. Its customer base includes Fortune 500 companies, government agencies, and technology firms, all seeking to enhance their cybersecurity through ethical hacking and continuous vulnerability discovery.

Hanshow Technology Co., Ltd. is a global leader in digital retail solutions, focusing on electronic shelf labels (ESL) and intelligent retail technologies. Founded in 2011/2012 and headquartered in Jiaxing, China, the company has expanded its presence with offices and subsidiaries in the U.S., Europe, and Asia. Hanshow aims to connect online and offline retail through innovative applications of IoT, AI, and big data.

The company offers a diverse range of products, including multiple generations of ESL, AI shelf monitoring systems, smart trolleys, digital signage, and an AIoT cloud platform. Their solutions are designed to enhance store operations, optimize inventory management, and improve customer engagement. Hanshow serves tens of thousands of stores across over 50 countries, partnering with major retail chains like Wu Mart in China. As a publicly listed company on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange, Hanshow continues to invest in research and development to drive growth and innovation in the retail technology sector.

Iglu.com is a UK-based online travel company that specializes in ski holidays and cruises. Founded in 1998, it has become the largest independent agent for ski holidays in the UK and one of the fastest-growing cruise travel agents. The company operates through three main brands: Iglu Ski, Iglu Cruise, and Planet Cruise, which was acquired in 2013 to enhance its cruise offerings.

Iglu.com provides access to over 90% of the UK ski package market and collaborates with all major cruise lines, ensuring a wide selection of holiday options. As an independent agent, it offers unbiased advice and competitive pricing. The company employs over 200 staff members who are experts in skiing and cruising, allowing for tailored customer service. Iglu.com emphasizes security and financial protection for its customers, ensuring reliable holiday bookings. With a reported turnover of around £90 million, the company continues to grow and expand its offerings.

Immersive is a cybersecurity company founded in 2017 and based in Bristol, UK, with additional locations in Boston, MA. The company focuses on providing a dynamic approach to cybersecurity training, moving beyond traditional classroom methods to equip professionals with practical skills for real-world cyber threats. 

The core offering is the Immersive One platform, which delivers challenge-based, scenario-driven content that simulates real cyber attack situations. This platform includes cyber drills, exercises, simulations, and training, all designed to enhance workforce resilience. Immersive's products cater to a wide range of users, including cybersecurity professionals and organizations, helping them improve their skills and operational readiness.

Immersive serves a diverse global customer base, including major enterprises and government organizations like Citi, Pfizer, and the UK Ministry of Defence. The company has raised approximately $189 million in funding, reflecting strong investor confidence in its innovative approach to cybersecurity training and resilience.

Kwalee Ltd. is a British video game developer and publisher, established in May 2011 by David Darling CBE. Headquartered in Leamington Spa, Warwickshire, the company also has offices in Bangalore, Beijing, and Portugal, employing over 180 staff worldwide. Kwalee specializes in creating and publishing games across various platforms, including mobile (iOS and Android), PC, and consoles, with a notable focus on hypercasual games that have achieved over 700 million downloads.

The company boasts a strong internal development studio and a growing division for PC and console games, led by industry veterans. Kwalee emphasizes innovation and creativity, providing employees with the latest technical equipment and opportunities for personal projects. The company promotes a positive work-life balance through activities like table tennis, social events, and flexible work arrangements, including remote work options. Overall, Kwalee is dedicated to delivering fun and accessible gaming experiences while fostering a supportive and dynamic work environment.

Labelbox is a San Francisco-based company founded in 2018 by Manu Sharma, Brian Rieger, and Daniel Chen. The company specializes in data-centric AI solutions, focusing on enhancing AI development through high-quality training data. Labelbox addresses the need for efficient and accurate data labeling, which is essential for training machine learning models.

The company offers a comprehensive platform that includes advanced annotation tools, workflow automation, and quality control systems. This SaaS platform supports teams in producing high-quality training data across various formats. Additionally, Labelbox provides a specialized data labeling service called Alignerr, which utilizes a network of subject matter experts for complex AI tasks. Their expert marketplace connects AI teams with skilled annotators, allowing for flexible scaling of data operations.

Labelbox serves a range of enterprise customers, including notable companies like Allstate and Bayer. The company has experienced rapid growth, reaching nearly $1 million in annual recurring revenue within its first year and continues to expand its offerings in the evolving AI ecosystem.

LMAX Group is a global financial technology company based in London, specializing in institutional execution venues for foreign exchange (FX) and digital assets trading. Founded in 2010, it operates in nine countries and serves clients across Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific. The company is known for its ultra-low latency and high-performance exchange infrastructure, with matching engines located in major financial centers like London, New York, Tokyo, and Singapore.

The company operates three main brands: LMAX Exchange, a regulated multilateral trading facility for institutional FX and commodities; LMAX Global, a broker providing access to deep institutional liquidity for retail brokers and professional traders; and LMAX Digital, a regulated cryptocurrency exchange for institutional clients. LMAX Group emphasizes transparency, efficient execution, and compliance, making it a trusted venue for trading in FX and digital assets. Its customer base includes institutional traders, retail brokers, and financial institutions seeking reliable market access.

Northamptonshire Children's Trust (NCT) is a public-sector organization dedicated to delivering effective children’s services in Northamptonshire, England. It collaborates with local unitary councils to enhance outcomes for children, young people, and families through strategic and solution-focused approaches. NCT emphasizes safeguarding and improving the lives of its service users, particularly vulnerable groups.

The Trust provides a range of integrated social care and therapeutic services, including trauma-informed support, mentoring programs, and foster care assistance. It focuses on mental health and wellbeing, especially for children in care and care-experienced young adults. NCT coordinates early help, public health, housing, and education services to offer holistic support to families. The organization is governed by a board and operates under the oversight of the Department for Education, ensuring accountability and effective resource allocation.

NCT is committed to empowering children and families with respect and compassion, striving to create an environment where best practices thrive. It has received recognition for its service quality and continues to work collaboratively with partners to improve the wellbeing and futures of children in Northamptonshire.

Ortec Finance is a global provider of quantitative modeling software and technology solutions, specializing in risk and return management for wealth management and institutional investment sectors. Founded in 1981 by econometrics students at Erasmus University Rotterdam, the company has grown to serve over 600 customers worldwide, supported by a team of around 260 experts across various international locations.

The company offers a range of solutions, including OPAL, a goal-based financial planning software that integrates with Salesforce, helping wealth managers align investment decisions with personal goals. Ortec Finance also develops advanced quantitative modeling and risk management tools tailored for both institutional and private markets. Additionally, its ClimateMAPS framework provides climate risk analysis, enabling financial institutions to assess the impacts of climate change on investments. With a strong customer base managing assets exceeding €14 trillion, Ortec Finance is recognized for its reliability and high client satisfaction.

Oxera Consulting LLP is an international economics and finance consultancy founded in 1982, with its main office in Oxford, UK, and branches in major European cities such as Brussels, Amsterdam, Berlin, Paris, Milan, and Rome. The firm specializes in providing expert advice on complex economic issues related to competition, regulation, finance, and public policy. Its diverse clientele includes policymakers, regulators, companies, and law firms.

Oxera offers a wide range of consultancy services, including competition economics, regulatory impact analysis, economic advice for litigation, policy formulation, and financial modeling. The firm is known for its analytical excellence and commitment to transparency, playing a significant role in shaping regulation and policy within the European Union. Oxera also produces detailed economic reports, proprietary economic models, and training programs, contributing to informed economic policy discussions. Its reputation for credibility makes it a trusted advisor to key decision-makers in both the public and private sectors.

Purcell is a prominent architectural practice founded in 1947, specializing in architecture, masterplanning, and heritage consultancy. With over 280 architects and heritage experts across 14 studios worldwide, including locations in the UK, Australia, and Hong Kong, Purcell focuses on protecting and enhancing significant historic buildings and places. The firm is recognized for its conservation-led approach, promoting adaptive reuse of existing structures to support sustainability and create vibrant community spaces.

As the world's largest architectural practice dedicated to the existing built environment, Purcell has been ranked No. 1 for heritage architecture in the World Architecture 100 for 2022 and 2023. The firm emphasizes a collaborative, employee-owned model that fosters personal development and social responsibility. Its services include architecture that integrates conservation with design, sustainable masterplanning, and expert heritage consultancy. Purcell's portfolio features a variety of projects, from the restoration of listed buildings to contemporary interventions that respect historical contexts while introducing modern functionality.

The Royal Agricultural University (RAU), located in Cirencester, Gloucestershire, England, is the oldest agricultural college in the English-speaking world, founded in 1845. It became a university in 2013 and has a rich heritage, supported by royal patronage since its inception. RAU focuses on agriculture, land management, and related rural disciplines, serving approximately 1,200 undergraduate students. The university also has campuses in Swindon and international locations, including China and Uzbekistan.

RAU offers a variety of undergraduate and postgraduate programs in agricultural sciences, rural land management, equine science, food business management, and environmental conservation. It is known for its pioneering role in agricultural education and provides professional development courses, research opportunities, and consultancy services. The university maintains strong connections with the agricultural sector, contributing to farming practices and education through research and knowledge transfer. RAU serves prospective students and the agricultural industry, positioning itself as a key institution for those pursuing careers in these fields.

S-RM is a global corporate intelligence and cybersecurity consultancy founded in 2005 and based in London. With around 250 employees across six international offices, the company specializes in intelligence, resilience, and response services. These services help clients manage complex risks, navigate competition, and address cyber, regulatory, and reputational threats.

The firm serves a diverse range of clients, including sovereign entities, multinational corporations, global law firms, ultra-high-net-worth individuals, private equity firms, and major investment banks. S-RM's core offerings include actionable intelligence for decision-making, enhancing organizational resilience against various risks, and rapid response to cyberattacks and crises. They also have specialized practices in disputes and investigations, deal advisory, strategic intelligence, and cybersecurity. S-RM utilizes proprietary AI-driven platforms to provide clients with real-time insights and strategic security analysis, ensuring tailored and effective solutions.

Aliases: Sensor Tower UK LTD, Sensor Tower UK, Sensor Tower
Description:
Sensor Tower is a global leader in mobile app and digital market intelligence, founded in 2013. The company offers a range of solutions, including App Intelligence, Store Intelligence, Ad Intelligence, and Usage Intelligence, which provide insights into app performance, marketplace trends, advertising strategies, and user engagement. With data on over 16 million apps, Sensor Tower helps marketers, app developers, and financial institutions understand user interactions and optimize their app growth strategies.

The company serves a diverse clientele, including Fortune 500 companies and game developers, with a presence in North America, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, APAC, and LATAM. Sensor Tower has regional offices in major cities like San Francisco, London, and Tokyo. Additionally, it produces detailed industry reports, such as the State of AI Apps Report, offering valuable market trends and insights for the mobile and digital advertising sectors.

Aliases: Stowe Family Law LLP, Stowe Family Law
Description:
Stowe Family Law LLP is the UK's largest specialist family law firm, established in 1982 and based in Leeds. With around 90 offices across major cities and market towns, the firm employs over 170 family lawyers who collectively bring more than 800 years of experience. Their team includes forensic accountants, adoption specialists, surrogacy law experts, and trained mediators.

The firm offers a wide range of services, including divorce and separation, financial settlements, child law, mediation, nuptial agreements, adoption law, surrogacy law, and international family law. Stowe Family Law is known for its technology-enabled approach to client acquisition, serving approximately 5,000 mass-affluent clients each year with a focus on care and empathy during family breakdowns. 

Founded by Marilyn Stowe, the firm has expanded significantly since its inception, transitioning to private equity ownership in 2017 and later to Investcorp in 2024, which has supported its growth and national coverage.

Company Name: The Thomas Coram Foundation for Children
Aliases: The Thomas Coram Foundation for Children
Description:
Coram is the UK's oldest dedicated children's charity, founded in 1739 as the Foundling Hospital by Thomas Coram. Over nearly 300 years, it has evolved into a comprehensive organization that supports children, young people, and families across the UK.

Coram offers a range of services, including adoption services to facilitate placements for children in need of families. It provides educational programs through Coram Life Education, delivering wellbeing and drug prevention education in over 2,000 schools. The organization also promotes children's rights through the Coram Children’s Legal Centre and influences adoption and fostering policy via CoramBAAF. Additionally, Coram preserves its rich heritage through projects that digitize historic archives and share its story with the public.

Coram is funded through school fees, donations, corporate support, and grants. Its mission is to improve the life chances of vulnerable children and families, making a significant impact in the community.

VVB Engineering is a UK-based contractor that specializes in the design, delivery, and maintenance of mechanical, electrical, fire, and telecommunication engineering services for infrastructure projects. Founded in 2018 and headquartered in London, the company employs around 300 staff and generates annual revenues of approximately $61.8 million. VVB Engineering combines the strengths of Tier 1 contractors with the agility of Tier 2 firms to provide tailored engineering solutions for sectors such as rail, highways, power, and tunnels.

The company offers a range of services, including mechanical engineering for HVAC and plumbing systems, electrical engineering for power generation and renewable energy, fire systems design and delivery, and telecommunications installation. VVB Engineering has been involved in notable projects such as the refurbishment of tunnels in Birmingham and the integration of video management systems at the Dartford Crossing. With a commitment to sustainable engineering and collaboration, VVB Engineering empowers the development of tomorrow's infrastructure.

Winckworth Sherwood LLP is a full-service law firm based in London, with a history that dates back to 1777. The firm has developed a strong reputation for providing expert legal advice across various sectors, including real estate, education, and parliamentary agency work. Winckworth Sherwood combines the qualities of a boutique firm with those of a larger City firm, offering both comprehensive legal services and specialized practices.

The firm serves a diverse clientele, including some of the UK's largest businesses, housebuilders, investors, and not-for-profit organizations. Winckworth Sherwood is known for its client-focused approach, delivering clear and practical legal advice that emphasizes understanding and empathy. The firm has established long-term relationships with clients, reflecting its commitment to high-quality service and professional excellence.

Zwift is a technology-driven entertainment company founded in 2014 in California. It specializes in creating an immersive virtual cycling and running platform that combines video game technology with fitness training. Users can ride or run indoors while interacting in virtual worlds, making workouts more engaging and social.

The core product is a software platform that connects to smart or conventional trainers, measuring power output and cadence to simulate real-world cycling conditions. Users can explore virtual environments, join group rides, participate in races, and follow structured workouts. Zwift also offers mobile and TV apps for controlling the experience and tracking progress across devices.

Targeting competitive cyclists and fitness enthusiasts, Zwift has grown significantly, with over 550,000 accounts reported as of early 2018. The company has attracted substantial investment to enhance its platform and expand into esports and broader fitness markets. Zwift merges fitness, gaming, and social interaction, creating a community-driven experience in indoor training.

Qodea is Europe's largest Google Cloud-only digital consultancy and transformation partner. The company specializes in providing cloud-based solutions and strategic consulting to help organizations maximize the benefits of Google Cloud technologies. Formed from the merger of Appsbroker and CTS, Qodea brings over two decades of experience in digital transformation, cloud security, data analytics, AI, and automation.

atomos wealth is a UK-based wealth management and financial planning company with headquarters in London and Bristol. Established in 1999, it specializes in financial intermediation and auxiliary financial services. The company offers tailored financial planning and investment management services aimed at helping individuals, families, and organizations achieve their financial goals, such as early retirement and supporting future generations.

Bloom & Wild is a prominent online flower and gift delivery company based in the UK, founded in 2013 by Aron Gelbard and Ben Stanway. The company is known for its innovative "letterbox flowers," which are fresh flowers packaged in slim boxes that fit through most letterboxes, allowing for convenient delivery. By sourcing flowers directly from producers in Kenya and Europe, Bloom & Wild ensures high-quality blooms that arrive in bud form, designed to last longer.

BLS International Services Ltd. is a leading service partner specializing in visa, passport, consular, and citizen services for governments and individuals. Founded in 2005 and based in New Delhi, India, the company operates in over 70 countries with more than 50,000 offices and a workforce of around 60,000 people. BLS International serves over 46 client governments and has processed more than 360 million applications.

Careline Lifestyles is a specialist care provider established in 1986 by Dr. Sharma, originally known as Durham Careline. The company focuses on delivering high-quality, person-centred care and support services for individuals with neurological, learning, and physical disabilities, as well as those with mental health needs and acquired brain injuries. It operates nine bespoke residential care homes across the North East of England, emphasizing the management of challenging behavior through BILD-accredited positive behaviour support plans.

Cosworth is a British automotive engineering company founded in 1958, known for its expertise in high-performance internal combustion engines and advanced propulsion technologies. Originally established as a racing engine maker, Cosworth gained recognition for its innovative DFV 3.0-liter V8 engine, which became the most successful Formula 1 engine in history. The company has expanded its offerings to include powertrain systems, electronics, and alternative propulsion technologies for various industries, including motorsport, automotive, aerospace, marine, and defense.

Earnix is a global software provider that specializes in AI-driven pricing, rating, underwriting, and personalization solutions for the insurance and banking industries. The company offers a real-time, cloud-based SaaS platform that integrates advanced analytics, machine learning, and generative AI. This platform helps financial services organizations optimize decision-making, enhance profitability, and accelerate their time to market.

Elite Group is a prominent provider of communications and technology solutions that help businesses operate more efficiently. The company offers a wide range of services, including consultancy, managed deployments, and end-to-end managed services. Its core offerings encompass unified communications, IT and cloud services, connectivity, and professional services, all designed to enhance collaboration and streamline operations.

Aliases: Fashion GPS Europe Ltd, Fashion GPS Europe
Description:
Launchmetrics is a data and technology company that specializes in software solutions for the Fashion, Lifestyle, and Beauty (FLB) industries. Founded in 2002 and headquartered in New York City, it has developed into a leading Brand Performance Cloud platform, serving over 1,700 brands in more than 100 countries. The company employs over 450 people across 12 global markets.

The company’s mission is to empower FLB brands to make informed decisions by integrating strategy with execution through digital transformation and data insights. Launchmetrics offers a comprehensive suite of tools, including sample management, event organization, press distribution, media monitoring, and brand performance benchmarking. These tools help brands streamline marketing processes, enhance operational efficiency, and improve overall business growth. With a focus on data-driven decision-making, Launchmetrics supports executives and professionals in the fashion, luxury, and beauty sectors in creating impactful brand experiences.

Foyle Food Group is a family-owned company with over 45 years of experience in the beef supply chain and processing. It is the largest dedicated beef processor in the UK and Ireland, operating five advanced facilities that can slaughter approximately 350,000 cattle annually and process around 180,000 cattle each year. The company emphasizes sustainable and regenerative practices, supporting local producers and participating in initiatives like the Aberdeen Angus Superior Genetics programme.

The group specializes in high-quality beef primals and related products, ensuring rigorous quality control and traceability from farm to fork. Their processing capabilities include slaughtering, deboning, further processing, dry aging, and retail packing. Foyle Food Group serves a diverse customer base, including retail, foodservice, and export markets, and is recognized as a leading beef exporter. With a workforce of over 1,350 people, the company values passion, quality, respect, and success, making its employees central to its achievements.

Hanwha Vision Europe is a subsidiary of Hanwha Vision, a leader in video surveillance technology and part of the Fortune Global 500 Hanwha Group based in South Korea. With over 30 years of expertise, the company specializes in advanced video surveillance solutions, including high-definition cameras, CCTV systems, and software that supports video analytics and management.

The company offers comprehensive end-to-end solutions that cover the entire project lifecycle, from data input and storage to processing and visualization. Hanwha Vision Europe emphasizes innovation, quality control, and cybersecurity, ensuring the integrity and privacy of surveillance operations. They provide extensive pre- and post-sales support, including system design, technical assistance, and training, along with a five-year warranty on their products. Their solutions cater to a wide range of customers, including public safety organizations and commercial enterprises, adapting to various security needs with scalable technology.

HealthHero is a leading digital healthcare provider in Europe, focused on simplifying healthcare and enhancing lives through advanced technology and clinical expertise. Founded in 2019 and based in London, the company has quickly become the largest telemedicine platform in Europe, serving over 22 million users in countries like the UK, Ireland, Germany, and France.

The company offers a wide range of virtual healthcare services available 24/7, including access to general practitioners, mental health support, digital prescriptions, and tools for managing chronic conditions. HealthHero emphasizes a whole-health approach, integrating technology with clinical services to provide preventive health tools and ongoing support. It serves various clients, including businesses, governments, and individual patients, aiming to improve healthcare accessibility and efficiency. HealthHero is also recognized as a member of the World Economic Forum’s Innovator Community, highlighting its commitment to enhancing global healthcare.

ICG Medical is a global healthcare recruitment company based in London, UK, founded in 2011. With over 250 employees and an annual turnover exceeding £54 million, the company specializes in recruiting healthcare professionals, particularly nurses, midwives, and home care workers. ICG Medical serves clients worldwide from its offices in various countries, including the UK, Ireland, India, South Africa, China, the Philippines, Japan, Australia, Canada, and the USA.

The company is dedicated to improving patient care by providing tailored staffing solutions for temporary, contract, and permanent recruitment needs. ICG Medical emphasizes building trusting relationships with candidates and clients, guided by its core values of openness, family orientation, and passion. Its services include global recruitment and placement, international relocation support for candidates, and specialized recruitment through multiple recognized brands targeting different healthcare sectors.

ITM Power is a UK-based company founded in June 2000, specializing in the design and manufacture of proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolysers for green hydrogen production. With over 25 years of research and development experience, ITM Power has become a pioneer in green hydrogen technology. The company’s advanced PEM electrolyser technology enables the production of green hydrogen by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, contributing to decarbonization efforts across various industries.

The company operates a high-volume manufacturing facility in Sheffield, England, with a current production capacity of 1 GW per annum and plans to expand to 5 GW by 2024. ITM Power offers modular PEM electrolysers that range from small-scale units to large industrial systems, including the world’s largest 24 MW PEM electrolyser. The company collaborates with major industrial partners like Linde plc and Sumitomo Corporation to develop and deploy electrolyser-based hydrogen energy systems, emphasizing its commitment to sustainability and global market expansion.

Larking Gowen is a prominent independent partnership of chartered accountants and business advisors, recognized as one of the UK's top 40 accountancy firms. With multiple offices in Norfolk, Suffolk, and Essex, including locations in Norwich, Ipswich, and Colchester, the firm provides a range of services tailored to various sectors.

The company offers comprehensive accounting and audit services, tax advisory, and business consultancy. They specialize in providing dedicated services to the medical sector, assisting healthcare professionals like General Practitioners and Practice Managers with their accounting and administrative needs. Larking Gowen emphasizes local expertise combined with global knowledge, ensuring clients receive tailored advice and practical solutions. Their focus on building close client relationships enhances accessibility and support for businesses and individuals in the region.

Maidstone Borough Council is the local government authority for Maidstone Borough in Kent, England. It serves residents, businesses, and visitors by providing a variety of local services and governance. The Council is led by 49 elected councillors and employs around 571 officers who work together to enhance community well-being.

The Council's strategic vision aims to make Maidstone a thriving place to live, work, and visit, focusing on job creation, housing needs, and infrastructure development. It offers services such as housing support, environmental services, community safety initiatives, and planning management. Additionally, the Council engages in climate transition efforts and manages local amenities like Cobtree Manor Estate and museums.

Maidstone Borough Council collaborates with local educational institutions and community groups to deliver its services effectively. Its commitment to governance, economic development, and environmental sustainability aims to improve the quality of life for all in the Maidstone area.

National Corporate Housing, founded in 1999 and based in Greenwood Village, Colorado, specializes in temporary furnished housing solutions. The company operates across the United States, Canada, Asia Pacific, Europe, and the Gulf region, focusing on high-quality customer service and customized housing options for both individual and corporate clients.

Their offerings include short-term furnished apartments for executives and individuals relocating, as well as destination services that assist clients in acclimating to new locations. National Corporate Housing also provides comprehensive rental assistance through licensed real estate agents, corporate travel management services, and talent solutions for businesses. Additionally, they have a dedicated team for insurance housing needs, ensuring rapid support for policyholders during claims or emergencies.

With a strong presence of over 276 employees and 28 offices, National Corporate Housing is recognized as a leader in the hospitality and corporate lodging industry, known for its commitment to quality service and employee satisfaction.

Opera North is England's national opera company based in Leeds, established in 1977. Originally known as English National Opera North, it became independent and adopted its current name in 1981. The company is recognized for its bold and innovative productions, performing over 220 shows annually at venues like Leeds Grand Theatre and Leeds Town Hall, as well as touring extensively throughout the UK and Europe.

The company stages a diverse repertoire that includes world premieres, rarely performed operas, and musical theatre works. Its orchestra, the Orchestra of Opera North, plays a unique role in both opera and concert performances. The Opera North Chorus is known for its innovative music-making. Additionally, Opera North engages with the community through educational programs and participative arts activities, promoting accessibility to opera for all ages. The company also curates a variety of artistic events, including gigs and film screenings, particularly at the Howard Assembly Room in Leeds.

Paysend is a global fintech company founded in the UK in 2017, specializing in cross-border money transfers through its digital payments network. It has grown from a startup focused on card-to-card transfers to a significant player in global payments, serving over 11 million consumers and numerous enterprise clients in more than 170 countries.

The company offers a range of services, including Paysend Consumer, which allows individuals to send and receive money instantly in over 170 countries. Paysend Enterprise provides a B2B solution for secure global payouts via a single API integration. Additionally, Paysend features a multicurrency account for managing multiple currencies, virtual cards for business payments, and is developing instant settlement accounts to enhance operational efficiency. With partnerships with major card networks like Mastercard and Visa, Paysend aims to simplify and reduce the costs of international transactions while promoting financial inclusion for both banked and unbanked populations.

Personetics is a fintech company based in New York, founded in 2011. It specializes in AI-driven cognitive banking solutions that help banks enhance and personalize customer engagement. By analyzing transaction data in real time, Personetics delivers actionable financial management insights to users.

The company's main offering is an AI-based engagement platform that provides banks with tools for expense tracking, budget management, cash flow forecasting, and financial wellness guidance. This technology allows banks to offer timely and relevant financial advice, fostering stronger customer loyalty and engagement. Personetics serves large global financial institutions, including both traditional banks and neobanks, and supports open banking initiatives, enabling secure data sharing for improved services. With over 150 million bank customers engaging monthly, Personetics generates hundreds of millions of personalized financial insights each year.

Aliases: Pragmatic Semiconductor Ltd, Pragmatic Semiconductor, Pragmatic
Description:
Pragmatic Semiconductor is a UK-based company located in Cambridge, specializing in the design and manufacture of flexible integrated circuits (FlexICs). Founded in 2010, the company focuses on creating ultra-low-cost, flexible microchip technology that allows for the embedding of intelligence into everyday objects. Their vision is to connect the physical and digital worlds through innovative semiconductor solutions, with a commitment to sustainability.

The core product, FlexICs, are ultra-thin integrated circuits that can be bent and embedded in various objects, such as product packaging. These chips utilize radio interface technology for seamless communication, enabling applications in smart packaging, wearables, and other emerging technologies. Pragmatic Semiconductor operates its own manufacturing facility in County Durham, ensuring quality control and scalability. The company targets global customers across industries that prioritize sustainability and smart technology integration.

Aliases: PURVIEW CONSULTANCY SERVICES LTD, PURVIEW CONSULTANCY SERVICES
Description:
Purview is a global IT company founded in 2010 by Mr. Punna Reddy. The company specializes in delivering innovative IT solutions and expert support, focusing on driving business growth and operational efficiency. With a multidisciplinary team of strategists, designers, technologists, and developers, Purview is dedicated to creating engaging digital experiences by integrating strategy, design, and technology.

Purview offers a wide range of services, including digital transformation, cybersecurity solutions, cloud services, and IoT and AI solutions. The company helps businesses adapt to the digital era, protect against cyber threats, optimize cloud strategies, and develop intelligent systems that enhance operational efficiency. Purview emphasizes quality, innovation, and client-centric solutions tailored to meet specific business needs, serving a diverse customer base across various industries.

Aliases: Reed Online Limited, Reed Online, Reed
Description:
Reed.co.uk is a prominent online recruitment platform based in the UK, established in 1995 as the first recruitment website by a recruitment agency. It is part of Reed, a family-run business founded in 1960, which has grown into one of the largest recruitment companies globally, with a presence in various regions including Europe, the USA, and East Asia.

The platform connects job seekers with employers and recruitment agencies, featuring a vast database of job vacancies and career resources. Reed.co.uk advertises job listings from nearly 19,000 recruiters annually and facilitates over 80,000 job applications each day. Additionally, it offers over 155,000 courses through Reed Courses, collaborating with around 1,400 learning providers. The site also provides extensive career advice, including tips on CV writing and interview preparation, attracting hundreds of thousands of visitors monthly. Reed.co.uk serves a diverse range of employers and educational course providers, making it a key resource for job seekers and recruiters across the UK.

Aliases: RG Carter Cambridge Ltd, RG Carter Cambridge
Description:
R G Carter is a construction company founded in 1921 by Robert George Carter, with a rich history of over 100 years. Based in Norfolk, UK, the company started by building local housing and has since expanded to undertake significant projects, including the construction of homes as part of the post-World War I "Homes for Heroes" scheme. 

The company provides a wide range of construction services, including design, build, refurbishment, repairs, and project management. It serves both public and private clients across various sectors such as housing, retail, commercial, and research. R G Carter is recognized as one of the largest Passivhaus housebuilders in the UK, reflecting its commitment to sustainable construction practices. With multiple regional offices, including a strong presence in Lincoln, the company emphasizes local community engagement and aims to improve lives through its projects. R G Carter values honesty, trust, and long-term relationships, combining traditional family values with innovation and quality craftsmanship.

The Ritz London is a prestigious luxury hotel located in Piccadilly, London, known for its rich history and exceptional service since opening in 1906. Founded by Swiss hotelier César Ritz, it has become a premier destination for guests seeking the highest standards of luxury and comfort. The hotel features opulent Edwardian architecture and interiors, with lavish furnishings and modern amenities that preserve classic style.

The Ritz offers luxurious guest rooms and suites, many named after notable past guests. It is famous for its traditional Afternoon Tea served in the elegant Palm Court and features The Ritz Restaurant, known for fine dining. The hotel also provides exquisite venues for private events, weddings, and corporate functions, ensuring impeccable service and grandeur. With a Royal Warrant granted by HRH The Prince of Wales, The Ritz London continues to cater to discerning guests, including international travelers, celebrities, and dignitaries, maintaining its status as a symbol of elegance and exclusivity.

Aliases: SIX DEGREES HOLDINGS LIMITED, SIX DEGREES HOLDINGS
Description:
Six Degrees is a UK-based technology company founded in 1995 and headquartered in London. It specializes in secure cloud services, managed IT solutions, and cybersecurity. As a leading Managed Service Provider (MSP), Six Degrees is dedicated to helping organizations operate effectively and securely in the cloud. The company aims to be the UK’s top provider of integrated cloud services, emphasizing a client-first approach and strong partnerships, particularly with Microsoft.

The company offers a range of services, including cloud managed services that optimize infrastructure, cybersecurity solutions for threat detection and response, and connectivity solutions that integrate data and voice services. Additionally, Six Degrees supports digital transformation by providing tailored solutions that align with clients' business goals. With a commitment to customer satisfaction, the company has received recognition for its quality of service and maintains a financially stable position with a turnover exceeding £54 million.

Star Refrigeration is the UK’s largest independent industrial refrigeration engineering company, founded in 1970 and based in Glasgow. With nearly 450 staff across 11 locations in the UK and agents in Europe and Asia, the company has developed into a multi-faceted engineering group. It specializes in designing, manufacturing, installing, and maintaining industrial refrigeration and heating systems.

The company provides custom-engineered refrigeration solutions for large-scale applications, pioneering natural refrigeration technologies, and energy optimization services. Star Refrigeration serves various industries, including food and drink processing, pharmaceuticals, and data centers. Known for its high-quality installations and exceptional aftercare, the company emphasizes energy efficiency and cost-effectiveness in its solutions. Notably, it developed the world’s largest zero carbon 90°C Ammonia District Heat Pump, recognized for its innovation in combating climate change.

Systemiq Ltd. is a collaborative system design and development company based in London, UK, founded in 2016. The company is dedicated to accelerating the transition to a sustainable economy, aligning its efforts with the Sustainable Development Goals and the Paris Agreement. Systemiq focuses on transforming five key economic systems: energy, nature and food, materials, urban areas, and finance.

The company offers a range of services, including system design and development, specialist advisory services, coalition building, leadership transformation, policy development, market and value chain redesign, capital mobilization, and support for early-stage ventures. Systemiq is a certified B Corporation, demonstrating its commitment to high standards of social and environmental performance. With a diverse international presence, Systemiq serves a wide array of clients, including governments, businesses, and financial institutions, all aiming to implement systemic change for improved environmental and social outcomes.

Thredd is an issuer processing company that modernizes payments for fintechs, digital banks, and embedded finance providers around the world. It processes billions of debit, prepaid, and credit transactions each year, serving over 100 clients in 44 countries across both consumer and corporate sectors.

The company provides a wide range of payment processing services, including authorization control, fraud detection, virtual cards, and rewards. Thredd supports various card types and offers advanced features like Visa Token Service, Visa Direct, and installment payments. It is recognized for its role in government disbursement programs, ensuring compliance and security for vulnerable beneficiaries. Founded in 2007 and based in London, Thredd combines technology with human expertise to assist businesses in becoming leaders in digital payments.

TransferGo is a London-based fintech company founded in 2012, specializing in international money transfers. It primarily serves migrant workers who send remittances to their families abroad. The company operates a digital remittance platform that offers fast, transparent, and cost-effective cross-border payments through online and mobile applications, avoiding traditional bank fees.

The core service of TransferGo is its account-to-account money transfer system, allowing senders to fund transfers from their local bank accounts, with recipients receiving funds directly into their bank accounts in over 160 countries. Transfers are often completed within 30 minutes, making it one of the fastest remittance services in Europe. TransferGo has expanded its offerings to include real-time payment capabilities and business payment solutions, catering to both individual and business clients.

With over 8 million customers globally by 2024, TransferGo emphasizes transparency and user experience, achieving high customer satisfaction ratings. The company is committed to social justice and economic empowerment, aiming to make financial services more accessible to migrants.

Audit Scotland is a public sector organization established in April 2000 under the Public Finance and Accountability (Scotland) Act 2000. It operates independently to provide assurance on the management and spending of public funds across Scotland, covering approximately £57 billion in public spending annually. The organization audits nearly 300 public sector accounts, including those of the Scottish Government, NHS, local authorities, and other public bodies.

Bulletproof is a global brand agency based in London, recognized as the largest independent brand agency in the world. It specializes in creative brand solutions that enhance growth, distinctiveness, and customer loyalty for a wide range of clients. Their impressive portfolio includes major international companies like Mondelēz International, Diageo, and The Heineken Company. Bulletproof combines creative expertise with a global reach to help clients build standout brands and foster strong customer connections.

CarFinance 247 is a leading online car finance broker based in Manchester, UK. The company connects car buyers, sellers, and lenders to simplify the car buying and financing process. As part of the 247 group, it operates as a credit broker and insurance intermediary, providing tailored finance options and insurance products to support customers throughout their car buying journey.

Corona Energy is a prominent independent energy supplier in the United Kingdom, with over 20 years of experience in serving business customers. Founded in 1996 and headquartered in Watford, Hertfordshire, the company specializes in supplying gas and electricity to the industrial and commercial sectors, holding about 14% of the UK's industrial and commercial gas market.

Cotswold Archaeology is a UK-based limited company and registered educational charity established in 1989. It focuses on advancing public education in archaeology and promoting appreciation of cultural heritage. The company has become one of the top five archaeological service providers in the UK, employing over 70 permanent staff and offering tailored archaeological solutions to both public and private sector clients.

Cowbell is a company focused on Adaptive Cyber Insurance, catering to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and middle-market businesses. Founded in 2019 and based in the San Francisco Bay Area, Cowbell utilizes advanced technology and artificial intelligence to offer cyber insurance that adapts to changing cyber threats. The company operates as a licensed Property & Casualty insurance producer across all 50 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, and the United Kingdom.

Curve is a financial technology company based in London, founded in 2015 by Shachar Bialick. It offers a banking platform that consolidates multiple bank cards, including debit, credit, and loyalty cards, into a single smart card and a user-friendly mobile app. This allows users to manage their finances more effectively without needing to switch banks.

David Zwirner is a contemporary art gallery founded in 1993 by David Zwirner in New York City. It has established itself as a global institution with locations in New York, Los Angeles, London, Paris, and Hong Kong. The gallery represents over seventy artists and estates, showcasing innovative exhibitions across various media and genres.

Duetto is a software-as-a-service (SaaS) company based in San Francisco, founded in 2010. It specializes in revenue management solutions for the hospitality industry, including hotels and casinos. Initially started as a consulting firm by former Wynn Resorts executives, Duetto has evolved into a cloud-native platform that simplifies distribution complexity and enhances profitability for its clients.

Duke Corporate Education (Duke CE) is a not-for-profit corporate education company affiliated with Duke University, established in 2000. It specializes in non-degree executive education and leadership development services, aiming to support organizations in achieving strategic transformation through customized learning solutions.

Eurostar is a prominent international high-speed rail company that connects major European cities, including London, Paris, Brussels, and Amsterdam. Since its inception in 1994, it has provided efficient rail services through the Channel Tunnel, linking the UK with continental Europe. Recently, Eurostar merged with Thalys, expanding its network to cover additional routes across the Benelux countries and Germany.

The company focuses on delivering high-speed passenger rail services that prioritize convenience, sustainability, and comfort. Eurostar offers various ticket classes, including Standard, Standard Premier, and Business Premier, along with amenities such as onboard Wi-Fi and business lounges. It also features a loyalty rewards program for frequent travelers. Eurostar operates from major stations in the UK and Europe, facilitating easy city-center travel and seasonal services to popular destinations. The company is committed to sustainability and has joined initiatives to combat climate change, making it a leader in environmentally friendly transport options.

Gedling Borough Council (GBC) is the local government authority for the north and northeast areas of Nottinghamshire, UK, serving approximately 114,000 residents across 21 wards. The council is dedicated to effective governance and community engagement, focusing on delivering a wide range of public services.

GBC provides essential services such as council tax collection, local elections management, and community consultations. It actively encourages citizen participation through initiatives like the Youth Council and various feedback channels. The council has embraced digital solutions to enhance service efficiency, including an online council tax billing system that has improved resident engagement and reduced operational costs.

With a strategic vision for the next three years, Gedling Borough Council aims to adapt to financial challenges while prioritizing community needs. The council is committed to fostering participation, enhancing productivity, and investing in technology to support its customer-centered approach.

Greenwood Academies Trust (GAT) is a not-for-profit multi-academy trust in England, established in 2009. It operates a network of 36 to 37 academies, educating approximately 17,000 to 19,000 pupils across several local authority areas, including Nottingham City, Leicester City, and Northamptonshire. The Trust is governed by a Board of Trustees and is dedicated to enabling every child within its academies to reach their full potential.

The Trust provides a range of support services through a Central Team, which includes finance, human resources, and curriculum development. Schools maintain significant autonomy over their budgets, with 94.5% controlled at the academy level. GAT emphasizes safeguarding and offers a staff bank and supply service, along with professional development opportunities for educational professionals. Its focus on raising educational standards and addressing underachievement is central to its mission, ensuring that pupils thrive and succeed in their educational journeys.

Hamberley Care Homes is a UK-based provider of luxury elderly care, operating 22 residential care facilities and 4 specialist care sites. Founded in 2018, the company focuses on delivering person-centred and holistic care, creating nurturing communities where residents can lead meaningful lives.

The services offered include residential care, nursing care, dementia care, respite care, neurocare, and rehabilitation. With 24-hour on-site nursing, Hamberley accommodates a variety of health needs and changes in care requirements. Their award-winning "Homemaker" model of care emphasizes a home-like atmosphere, promoting warmth and engagement while allowing residents to maintain their lifestyle choices and independence.

Hamberley Care Homes targets elderly individuals and their families seeking premium care that combines comfort and expert clinical support. The company has been recognized as Residential Care Provider of the Year in 2019 and 2022 by Health Investor, reflecting its innovative approach to elderly care.

Hanson Wade is a global conference and event organizing company based in London, founded in 2008. With around 170 employees, it specializes in creating industry-specific events and tailored content for sectors such as healthcare, life sciences, finance, technology, and energy. The company aims to connect professionals within these niche industries to foster innovation and facilitate knowledge sharing.

Hanson Wade organizes a variety of events, including conferences, seminars, workshops, and networking sessions. It also produces specialized products that address research and development needs, particularly in life sciences. The company manages industry-leading summits and maintains year-round community engagement to support ongoing connections and knowledge exchange. Recognized for its meritocratic culture, Hanson Wade emphasizes ambition and professional development, delivering relevant content and networking opportunities that empower better decision-making and accelerate business performance.

iBanFirst is a prominent European fintech company that specializes in international cross-border payment solutions for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMBs). Founded in 2016 by Pierre-Antoine Dusoulier, the company has grown rapidly, employing over 350 people and serving more than 10,000 clients from 13 offices across Europe, including its headquarters in Brussels.

The company offers a comprehensive financial services platform that simplifies international payments. iBanFirst provides personalized International Bank Account Numbers (IBANs) in over 25 currencies, multi-currency accounts, real-time exchange rates, and payment tracking. Their services also include custom hedging strategies to manage foreign exchange risk. iBanFirst is licensed by the National Bank of Belgium and processes over €1.4 billion in transactions monthly, making it a trusted partner for businesses engaged in international trade. The company values trust, integrity, and transparency, fostering a collaborative work environment across its diverse team.

The International Planned Parenthood Federation (IPPF) is a global non-governmental organization established in 1952 in Mumbai, India. Headquartered in London, IPPF is dedicated to promoting sexual and reproductive health and advocating for individuals' rights to make informed choices about family planning. The organization operates through over 149 Member Associations in more than 189 countries, focusing on accessible healthcare for all.

IPPF provides a variety of services, including comprehensive sex education, contraceptive distribution, safe abortion and maternal health services, and training on sexual health and abuse prevention. A significant portion of its resources is directed toward young people under 25, who are at higher risk of HIV infection. The organization emphasizes community outreach and volunteer-driven healthcare delivery, ensuring that services are culturally appropriate and accessible, particularly for low-income populations.

In addition to service provision, IPPF advocates for sexual and reproductive health and rights globally, collaborating with international bodies and governments to promote these rights as fundamental human rights.

Mangopay is a payment solution provider that specializes in payment infrastructure for marketplaces, crowdfunding platforms, and fintech companies. Founded in 2013, it offers a flexible e-wallet ecosystem designed to facilitate global money movement and manage complex payment flows efficiently and securely.

The company provides e-wallet accounts for holding and managing user funds, payment acceptance solutions for various methods, global payouts for cross-border transfers, and integrated foreign exchange services. Its programmable wallet infrastructure supports fraud prevention and customizable payment flows tailored to the needs of its clients. Mangopay serves notable platforms like Wallapop, Vinted, and Milanuncios, particularly in the Spanish market.

Regulated as an electronic money institution in Luxembourg, Mangopay has processed over 100 billion euros in transactions and serves more than 250 million end-users worldwide. With offices in several European cities, including Paris, Warsaw, and Madrid, the company is focused on expanding its presence and enhancing its payment solutions for global platforms.

MUBI is a global media company founded in 2007 by Efe Cakarel, dedicated to bringing the world's greatest films to audiences through a curated experience. Originally launched as TheAuteurs.com, it rebranded to MUBI in 2010. The company operates a streaming platform available in over 190 countries, featuring a carefully selected catalogue of arthouse, independent, and world cinema films.

In addition to streaming, MUBI engages in film production and theatrical distribution, supporting both emerging and established filmmakers in key markets like the US, UK, and Germany. The company also publishes *Notebook*, a film criticism and news publication, and offers cinema tickets to new-release films through its MUBI GO program. MUBI has expanded into original content with podcasts in various languages, collaborating with industry figures to enhance the film experience for its audience of cinephiles and film lovers.

Company Name: North Cumbria Integrated Care NHS Foundation Trust
Description:
North Cumbria University Hospitals NHS Trust was an NHS acute hospital trust that served around 340,000 people in northern Cumbria, including areas like Allerdale, Carlisle, Copeland, and Eden. Established in 2001 through the merger of Carlisle Hospitals NHS Trust and West Cumbria Healthcare NHS Trust, it gained University Hospital status in 2008. The Trust operated primarily from Cumberland Infirmary in Carlisle and West Cumberland Hospital in Whitehaven, with additional maternity services at Penrith Hospital.

The Trust provided a wide range of services, including emergency care, maternity care, children’s services, surgery, critical care, cancer services, and outpatient clinics. In 2019, it merged with Cumbria Partnership NHS Foundation Trust to form North Cumbria Integrated Care NHS Foundation Trust (NCIC). This new organization offers integrated hospital, community, and mental health services to approximately 500,000 people across Cumbria, employing over 6,500 staff and delivering more than 70 services.

Pro-Force Limited is a UK-based recruitment agency established in 2005, located in Chartham, Canterbury, Kent. With over 250 employees and an annual turnover exceeding £54 million, the company has built a strong reputation by providing tailored recruitment services that meet the specific needs of its clients.

The agency specializes in temporary employment and seasonal worker placements, participating in the Seasonal Worker Scheme to extend its recruitment reach across the UK, EU, and globally. Pro-Force offers bespoke recruitment models across various sectors, ensuring that clients receive the right talent for roles ranging from baking to driving. The company is dedicated to continuous improvement in its recruitment practices, aiming to deliver high-quality services to both clients and job seekers.

Pro-Force values trust, reliability, and agility, which guide its operations and partnerships. The leadership team, including Finance Director Patricia Mary Cox and Director Matthew James Jarrett, is committed to fostering innovation in recruitment methods and supporting a diverse client base across multiple industries.

The Professional Squash Association (PSA) is the international governing body for professional squash, overseeing both men's and women's competitions globally. Established in 1975, the PSA has played a vital role in shaping the sport for over 50 years. It manages the PSA Squash Tour, which features more than 250 tournaments annually across over 60 countries, with over 1,200 registered professional players.

The PSA provides essential services, including tournament management, a computerized ranking system for players, and enforcement of a code of conduct. It also oversees a Satellite Tour for national championships and collaborates with sponsors like Dunlop to promote the sport. The organization works closely with tournament organizers, national squash associations, and media to enhance the visibility and popularity of squash worldwide. Headquartered in Leeds, England, the PSA is led by Ziad Al-Turki and CEO Alex Gough.

Company Name: See-Ability Ltd t/aThe Royal School For The Bllind
Aliases: See-Ability Ltd t/aThe Royal School For The Bllind
Description:
SeeAbility is a UK-based charity founded in 1799, recognized as one of the oldest disability organizations in the world. It specializes in supporting individuals with learning disabilities, autism, and sight loss. The charity's mission is to empower those they support to lead ambitious lives, challenge societal expectations, and achieve personal goals, including forming relationships and gaining employment.

The organization offers tailored support and residential services, ensuring individuals can live fulfilling and independent lives. SeeAbility also runs eye care programs that provide essential services, such as eye testing and vision information, particularly for those with learning disabilities who are at higher risk of sight problems. Their advocacy efforts include campaigns for improved eye care policies, notably the *Children in Focus* campaign, which has influenced NHS policy regarding eye and dental testing in special schools.

Through its programs, resources, and advocacy, SeeAbility aims to enhance the lives of its beneficiaries while promoting awareness and systemic change for better inclusion and support for people with disabilities.

Aliases: Stoke City Football Club Ltd, Stoke City Football Club
Description:
Stoke City Football Club is a professional football club located in Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England. Founded in 1863 as Stoke Ramblers, the club became Stoke City in 1925 when the city was granted status. As one of the oldest clubs in England, Stoke City was a founding member of the Football League in 1888 and is nicknamed *The Potters*, reflecting the local pottery industry.

The club competes in the EFL Championship and plays its home matches at the bet365 Stadium, which has a capacity of around 30,000. Stoke City has a rich history, including winning the Football League Cup in 1972 and the Football League Trophy twice. The club is known for its local rivalries, particularly with Port Vale, and engages in various community outreach initiatives and youth development programs. Fans can enjoy matchday experiences, including ticket sales and hospitality packages, as well as purchase official merchandise.

Stride Treglown is a prominent architectural and design practice based in the UK, founded in 1953. With headquarters in Bristol and additional offices in the UK and the UAE, it is one of the largest firms in the country, employing over 300 specialists and generating around $57.3 million in revenue.

The company provides a wide array of services, including architecture, building information modeling (BIM), interior design, landscape architecture, project management, and sustainability consulting. Stride Treglown emphasizes a human-centered approach, fostering collaboration among clients, consultants, and contractors to achieve design excellence. The firm serves a diverse range of sectors, including healthcare, education, retail, and community projects, and is known for notable transformations such as the refurbishment of historic buildings and the creation of low-carbon community spaces.

Recognized for its technical proficiency, Stride Treglown utilizes advanced technologies like 3D modeling and virtual reality visualization in its design processes. The firm is also a certified B Corp, highlighting its commitment to positive social and environmental impact.

Aliases: T & L Sugars Limited, T & L Sugars
Description:
Tate & Lyle Sugars is a historic British company specializing in the refining and production of cane sugar and related syrup products. Established in 1921 through the merger of Henry Tate & Sons and Abram Lyle & Sons, the company has deep roots in the UK sugar market, historically refining around 50% of the nation's sugar. It operates multiple refineries and manufacturing sites in Europe, supported by a global supply chain.

The company is known for its commitment to sustainability and ethical sourcing in sugar refining. Its product range includes refined cane sugars and syrups, with Golden Syrup being a flagship product recognized for its heritage. Tate & Lyle Sugars serves food manufacturers, retailers, and consumers who appreciate high-quality sugar and syrup products, which are commonly used in baking, cooking, and food production. The company emphasizes quality, tradition, and environmental stewardship in its operations.

AKT II is a design-led engineering practice based in London, UK, with a team of over 350 professionals. Founded in 1996, the firm specializes in structural, civil, and transportation engineering consulting services. It operates globally, reaching more than 50 countries, and is recognized as one of the largest structural engineering consultancies in London.

AMICULUM is an independent global healthcare communications, consulting, and learning company based in Bollington, United Kingdom. With a workforce of approximately 200-500 employees and annual revenue around $51 million, the company focuses on activating science through insights-driven narratives and experiences. They aim to bring clients' scientific data and brands to life for various healthcare audiences, including healthcare professionals, payers, patients, and advocates.

Blue Zoo Animation Studio is a British animation company founded in 2000 by Bournemouth University graduates Tom Box, Oli Hyatt, and Adam Shaw. It has grown from a small setup in a garage to a leading UK animation studio with over 300 staff. The studio has received multiple prestigious awards, including five BAFTAs, and is recognized for its commitment to creative excellence and technical innovation.

Carpmaels & Ransford LLP is a distinguished European intellectual property law firm with a history that dates back to 1776. As one of the oldest firms in the IP sector, it has evolved into a full-service provider, managing all aspects of intellectual property across Europe. The firm specializes in a wide range of IP services, including patent and trademark applications, license agreements, property rights transfer, and litigation.

CreatorIQ is a leader in influencer marketing software, founded in 2014 and based in Culver City, California, with additional offices in the US and Europe. The company offers an enterprise-grade platform designed to help brands and agencies scale and optimize their creator marketing programs effectively.

DMH Stallard LLP is a full-service law firm established in 1970 in Brighton, originally known as Donne Mileham & Haddock. With over 50 years of experience, the firm has expanded its presence across Sussex, Surrey, and London, operating offices in key locations such as London, Brighton, Gatwick, Guildford, and Horsham. Since becoming a Limited Liability Partnership in 2008, DMH Stallard has built a strong reputation for providing clear and accessible legal advice tailored to both businesses and individuals.

Exclaimer is a UK-based information technology company that specializes in email signature management software and cloud computing solutions. Founded in 2001 and headquartered in Farnborough, the company focuses on helping organizations manage and automate email signatures, disclaimers, and branding across corporate email systems. Exclaimer's solutions integrate seamlessly with Microsoft Exchange Server, Microsoft 365, and Google Workspace.

The company offers both on-premises and cloud-based email signature management solutions, ensuring consistent branding and compliance for businesses of all sizes. Exclaimer also provides customer satisfaction tools through its acquisition of Customer Thermometer in 2021.
