Founded in 2004, the company serves over 120 customers in more than 60 countries, processing over 4 billion transactions each year. Provenir's platform integrates data, artificial intelligence, and automated decisioning tools to help banks, fintechs, and financial services providers make informed decisions throughout the customer lifecycle.

The platform supports various functions, including customer onboarding, fraud and identity verification, and collections. It optimizes credit risk and compliance decisioning with AI models that continuously improve accuracy. Provenir's low-code, scalable platform allows financial institutions to deploy machine learning models and adapt to changing business needs efficiently. The technology enables organizations to automate a significant portion of their decisions, enhancing operational efficiency and reducing costs. Provenir's diverse customer base includes notable financial institutions like GM Financial, Sofi, and BBVA, all leveraging its solutions to streamline lending processes and improve decision-making speed.

Stephensons Solicitors LLP is a full-service law firm based in the UK, established in 1977 and headquartered in Wigan, Greater Manchester. With over 450 staff across seven offices, the firm serves more than 25,000 clients each year. Recognized as a top 150 law firm in the UK, Stephensons has achieved tier-one status in the Legal 500 and holds the Investors In People Gold standard, highlighting its commitment to staff development and management excellence.

The firm offers a wide range of legal services, including personal injury claims, family law, corporate law, and commercial property law. Its largest office in Wigan features specialized teams in various legal disciplines, ensuring comprehensive support for both individual and business clients. Stephensons is regulated by the Solicitors Regulation Authority, ensuring adherence to professional standards and client protections. With a strong reputation for quality and innovation, the firm is dedicated to providing effective legal solutions tailored to meet the needs of its diverse clientele.

Synectics Solutions is a UK-based company that specializes in fraud prevention, financial crime risk analysis, and risk management software and services. Founded in 1992 and headquartered in Newcastle Under Lyme, Staffordshire, the company has over 30 years of experience and operates in 16 countries. With a workforce of around 338 employees, Synectics generates approximately $52.8 million in revenue and maintains a strong client retention rate of 98%.

The company offers a range of advanced solutions, including the SIRA Decisioning Platform for real-time fraud detection, the SYNID Digital ID Verification Tool for improving customer approval rates, and ECCTA Compliant Employee Screening to mitigate insider fraud risks. Synectics also provides a Risk Assessment Lab to help clients understand their fraud risk landscape. Their services include detailed delivery scoping, data integration, and training to empower clients in managing their fraud strategies effectively. Synectics Solutions is recognized for its commitment to security and compliance, being fully ISO27001 certified and designated as a UK Government Specified Anti-Fraud Organisation.

Talon Outdoor is a global, independent full-service Out of Home (OOH) agency founded in 2013. The company operates in over 100 markets worldwide and focuses on transforming the OOH sector with innovative, technology-led solutions that blend creativity with measurable results.

Talon offers a variety of services, including campaign planning and execution using proprietary AdTech platforms, creative solutions through its award-winning studio Grand Visual, and data-driven measurement and analysis to ensure effective campaigns. The company fosters collaboration with leading agencies, advertisers, and media owners, enhancing its ability to serve a diverse client base across various industries.

Driven by a mission to grow the OOH sector, Talon emphasizes independence, collaboration, and innovation, aiming to redefine OOH advertising as a highly effective and sustainable platform for clients.

Aliases: The Careers & Enterprise Company, The Careers & Enterprise
Description:
The Careers & Enterprise Company (CEC) is a national body in England, established in 2015, focused on enhancing careers education in schools and colleges. Its mission is to assist young people in transitioning from education to employment or further training. CEC provides resources, tools, and training to improve the quality of careers education, aligning with the Gatsby Benchmarks.

CEC connects employers with educational institutions through a network of over 4,000 volunteer Enterprise Advisers and around 300 Cornerstone Employers. This collaboration aims to develop impactful careers programs. The organization emphasizes inclusion by offering digital tools and resources for young people with special educational needs and disabilities. Additionally, CEC drives local partnerships through Careers Hubs, aligning careers education with local economic needs and promoting apprenticeships and vocational pathways.

The company offers training for Careers Leaders, digital tools for data-driven planning, and employer engagement programs to facilitate meaningful interactions between students and the workforce. CEC serves schools, colleges, employers, and local authorities, fostering collaboration to ensure high-quality careers education for all young people.

Aliases: United National Bank Limited, United National Bank
Description:
UBL UK, officially known as United National Bank Limited, is a UK-based banking institution established in 2001 through the merger of the UK branches of United Bank Limited and National Bank of Pakistan. It operates as a private limited company and is fully authorized and regulated by the Prudential Regulation Authority and the Financial Conduct Authority in the UK.

The bank offers a variety of services, including retail banking products like current and savings accounts, as well as remittance services. It also provides wholesale banking solutions for corporate clients, such as trade finance and business loans. UBL UK specializes in money transmission services, particularly for the Pakistani diaspora in the UK, facilitating cross-border financial transactions and supporting individuals and businesses with ties to Pakistan. The bank focuses on maintaining financial connectivity between the UK and Pakistan, serving both personal and corporate banking needs.

Company Name: Bistrot Pierre 1994 Limited T/A Bistrot Pierre
Description: Bistrot Pierre is a UK-based Anglo-French restaurant chain founded in 1994 by Robert Beacham and John Whitehead. Originally a franchise of Pierre Victoire in Nottingham, it became an independent group and rebranded as Le Bistrot Pierre in 2005. The company operates around 16-17 locations across England and Wales, focusing on delivering great value French cooking with seasonal menus.

Carbon13 is a venture builder and investor focused on tackling the climate emergency by creating and supporting impactful climatetech startups. Founded in Cambridge, UK, and now also based in Berlin, the company aims to harness entrepreneurial talent to develop ventures that can significantly reduce carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) emissions at scale. Their mission includes supporting 1,000 entrepreneurs over five years to create ventures capable of mitigating over 400 million tonnes of CO2e emissions annually.

Place is a real estate technology company that empowers high-performing real estate agents with an all-in-one platform designed to streamline their business operations. Founded in 2020, Place operates in over 100 markets across the United States and Canada, focusing on the top 20 percent of agents who handle the majority of real estate transactions.

The Development Bank of Wales is a public limited company owned by the Welsh Government, dedicated to enhancing the economic landscape in Wales. Established in 2000 and rebranded in 2017, it serves as a strategic asset aimed at fostering long-term economic growth and job creation. Headquartered in Wrexham, with offices in Cardiff, Llanelli, and St Asaph, the bank employs around 275 people and manages over £1.4 billion in funds.

Drees & Sommer UK is a multidisciplinary consultancy focused on the built environment, emphasizing sustainability and innovation. As part of the global Drees & Sommer group, which has over 5,100 employees worldwide, the UK branch operates from seven key locations and offers a wide range of services. These include project management, cost management, facilities management consultancy, real estate investment and development consultancy, building surveying, health and safety auditing, and sustainability consulting.

Aliases: Ernest Doe & Sons Ltd, Ernest Doe & Sons
Description:
Ernest Doe & Sons Ltd is a family-owned machinery dealership based in Ulting, Essex, with a history dating back to 1898. Originally a blacksmith’s shop, the company has grown into one of the UK’s largest dealers, operating 21 branches across East Anglia and the South East. 

The company offers a diverse range of machinery and equipment, including agricultural machinery like tractors and combine harvesters, construction machinery such as excavators, and groundcare equipment including lawn mowers and garden tools. They also provide power tools, outdoor clothing, and Calor Gas appliances. Ernest Doe & Sons Ltd is known for its expert support and comprehensive service facilities to maintain and repair machinery.

Operating under two main brandings, Ernest Doe focuses on agricultural machinery with major franchises like New Holland Agriculture, while Ernest Doe Power specializes in agricultural and power machinery, including Case IH Agriculture. The company serves a wide customer base, including farmers, construction companies, and homeowners.

Aliases: Four Agency Worldwide Limited, Four Agency Worldwide
Description:
Four Agency Worldwide is an independent media, marketing, and communications agency with over 20 years of experience. With a team of around 350 employees, the agency operates from seven offices in the UK and the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. Formerly known as Four Communications Group, it rebranded to better reflect its global scale and integrated service offerings.

The agency specializes in integrated communications solutions that encompass advertising, digital marketing, public relations, and events management. It adopts a sector-led approach, focusing on areas such as health, travel, financial services, and government. By combining expertise from various fields, Four Agency Worldwide creates impactful campaigns tailored to resonate with diverse audiences. Its services include media planning, strategic insights, and integrated marketing consultancy, allowing for comprehensive campaigns that drive behavior change and enhance brand visibility.

Hall & Partners is a global insight agency that specializes in brand strategy, brand performance, and campaign effectiveness research. Founded over thirty years ago by Mike Hall, the company aims to align research with the strategic goals of brands through a philosophy of "creativity with discipline." 

The agency offers bespoke, data-driven insights that combine creativity with scientific rigor. Their core services include measuring and building brand equity, developing differentiated strategies, and evaluating marketing campaigns to enhance customer engagement. Hall & Partners is recognized for its flexible and responsive approach, blending the resources of a large agency with the personalized care of a smaller firm.

Headquartered in London, Hall & Partners employs nearly 400 people and generates around $153 million in revenue. The company has a strong reputation for working with some of the world’s leading brands, providing strategic frameworks and creative thinking that influence advertising planning globally. In 2023, Hall & Partners joined Escalent Group, enhancing its capabilities and industry expertise.

Heatherwick Studio is a British design and architecture firm founded in 1994 by Thomas Heatherwick. Headquartered in London, with additional offices in Shanghai and Singapore, the studio operates globally across Asia, Europe, North America, and the Middle East. It is recognized for its innovative, human-centered design approach that integrates architecture, urban planning, product design, and public art, emphasizing sustainability and social value.

The studio's portfolio includes notable projects such as The Learning Hub at Nanyang Technological University in Singapore, the Bombay Sapphire Distillery in the UK, and the Rolling Bridge in London. Other significant works include the Zeitz Museum of Contemporary Art Africa in Cape Town, Little Island park in New York, and Coal Drops Yard in London. Heatherwick Studio collaborates on large-scale projects like the new Changi Airport Terminal 5 in Singapore and Google’s campuses in London and California, often partnering with other leading architectural firms. Their diverse clientele includes public institutions, cultural organizations, and global corporations, all benefiting from the studio's commitment to innovative design and socially valuable environments.

The Institute of Contemporary Music Performance (ICMP) is a specialist music education institution in the UK, with campuses in London and Liverpool. Established for over 35 years, it is the longest-running contemporary music school in the country. ICMP focuses on preparing musicians, producers, performers, and entrepreneurs for successful careers in the creative and music industries through tailored, industry-aligned education.

ICMP offers a variety of programs, including undergraduate degrees, postgraduate Masters programs, Higher Education Certificates, and short courses. These programs cover a range of topics such as music performance, production, songwriting, and music business. The curriculum emphasizes practical experience, with opportunities for live performances and collaborations, and maintains strong connections with industry professionals to enhance networking and career prospects for students. ICMP serves aspiring musicians and music professionals from the UK and around the world, providing a supportive environment for skill development and career advancement.

UNiDAYS is a global technology company founded in 2011 and headquartered in Nottingham, UK, with offices in Silicon Valley, New York, Sydney, and London. It operates as the leading student affinity network, offering a platform that provides verified student discounts and benefits from over 800 partner brands worldwide.

The core service of UNiDAYS is its student discount platform, which allows eligible students to access exclusive discounts on various products and services, including clothing, technology, food, and gym memberships. The platform quickly verifies student eligibility, often within minutes, ensuring a smooth user experience. With a mobile app and website that have millions of users and a high rating, UNiDAYS makes it easy for students to shop online and save money.

UNiDAYS aims to support and inspire young people by enhancing their learning and retail experiences through digital identity. The company partners with major global brands and provides insights into Gen Z consumer behavior, helping brands connect with this demographic effectively.

Aliases: Orbital Express Launch Limited, Orbital Express Launch
Description:
Orbex, officially known as Orbital Express Launch Ltd., is a UK-based aerospace company founded in 2015. Headquartered in Forres, Scotland, with additional facilities in Denmark and subsidiaries in Germany, Orbex specializes in low-cost, sustainable orbital launch services for the small satellite industry. The company is recognized as one of Europe’s leading private launch service providers.

The flagship product, Orbex Prime, is a micro-launch vehicle designed for small satellite launches. It utilizes renewable bio-propane fuel, significantly reducing the carbon footprint compared to traditional rocket fuels. The rocket features innovative technologies such as 3D-printed engines and carbon fiber structures, enhancing both performance and sustainability. Orbex emphasizes environmental responsibility by minimizing space debris and maximizing reusability.

Orbex offers a flexible core launch service with supporting services to simplify the launch process for customers. The company plans to operate primarily from the Sutherland Space Hub in northern Scotland, a carbon-neutral spaceport, and may expand to other locations in the future. Orbex serves small satellite operators seeking reliable and environmentally conscious launch solutions, supported by funding from public and private sectors.

Aliases: Realterm Europe Management UK Ltd, Realterm Europe Management UK
Description:
Realterm is a global investment manager based in Annapolis, Maryland, specializing in transportation-focused real estate and infrastructure assets. Founded in 1991, the company manages a diverse portfolio of over 400 investments worldwide, supporting land, air, sea, and rail transportation networks. With a team of more than 250 professionals, Realterm is dedicated to enhancing the global supply chain and logistics sectors.

The company’s core activities include acquiring, developing, financing, and managing logistics properties and infrastructure that facilitate efficient transportation. Realterm focuses on high-flow-through logistics properties, aiming to generate value for investors and strengthen communities. Their services encompass investment management, development and financing of transportation-related properties, active asset management, and fund management through private equity vehicles.

Realterm's portfolio includes logistics facilities, warehouses, and infrastructure assets that cater to the transportation industry. They prioritize building long-term relationships with institutional investors and other stakeholders to deliver sustainable growth and operational performance.

Royal Trinity Hospice is England's oldest hospice, established in 1891 by William Hoare. Located in Clapham, London, it serves seven boroughs and specializes in palliative care, focusing on symptom relief and enhancing the quality of life for patients with life-limiting illnesses. The hospice offers both inpatient and outpatient services, featuring 28 beds in a Georgian terrace complemented by modern facilities, including therapeutic gardens and a contemporary pavilion.

The hospice is dedicated to providing compassionate care, allowing families to spend meaningful time with patients through flexible policies, such as overnight stays and visits from pets. Funding for the hospice is approximately £12 million annually, with a significant portion raised through community fundraising and a network of 25 charity shops in southwest London. Royal Trinity Hospice combines clinical excellence with a patient-centered approach, ensuring support for both patients and their families.

Sport Aberdeen is a registered charity established in 2010, dedicated to enhancing lives through sport and physical activity in Aberdeen, Scotland. As the largest sport and leisure provider in the north-east of Scotland, it manages over 30 venues and employs more than 500 staff, facilitating around two million customer visits each year. 

The organization focuses on increasing participation in sports for people of all ages, genders, and abilities, with a strong emphasis on inclusivity. Sport Aberdeen reinvests all profits back into the community, ensuring that its programs and facilities are accessible to everyone. Its services include operating sports facilities, delivering community sport programs, and promoting health and wellbeing initiatives. The headquarters is located at Broadfold House in Aberdeen, where it coordinates its extensive activities to foster a healthier, more active community.

Stace is an independent construction and property consultancy based in the UK, established in 1959. The company offers a wide range of services, including Project Management, Cost Management, Building Surveying, Health & Safety, Monitoring Surveying, and Due Diligence. They also provide Employer’s Agent, Contract Administration, and Party Wall services. Stace prioritizes collaboration with clients to achieve their goals, which is reflected in their impressive client retention rate of 85%.

With a history of over 60 years, Stace has expanded its operations to include offices in Cambridge, Epping, Leamington Spa, Leeds, and London. The firm has participated in notable projects across the UK, including iconic London landmarks and regional developments like the Victoria Shopping Arcade in Leeds. Recognized as one of the top 20 UK consultancies, Stace is also the largest SME surveying practice in the country, operating as a limited liability partnership with a dedicated team of under 250 employees.

Climate Group is a global nonprofit organization established in 2004, dedicated to accelerating climate action and achieving net zero carbon emissions by 2050. Headquartered in the United Kingdom, it operates internationally with offices in Europe, North America, India, and China. The organization builds influential networks of businesses and governments to promote collective action and implement solutions across key sectors, including energy, transport, and food systems.

As the secretariat for the Under2 Coalition, Climate Group supports 178 subnational governments in their commitment to reducing greenhouse gas emissions. The organization provides technical assistance, capacity building, and advocacy to help these members develop effective climate action plans. Additionally, Climate Group partners with business coalitions to enhance corporate climate commitments and facilitate the adoption of renewable energy and low-emission materials. Through its initiatives, Climate Group plays a vital role in driving systemic change toward a sustainable, zero emissions economy.

Union Maritime Ltd. is a UK-based private limited company and a leader in the maritime shipping industry, focusing on the ownership and management of a modern fleet of tanker vessels. Established in 2006 and headquartered in Wembley, Greater London, the company operates over 70 vessels, making it one of the youngest fleets in operation.

The company provides a wide range of services, including commercial management through its Union Commercial Services division, which manages both its owned fleet and third-party assets. Union Maritime also offers technical management to ensure vessel efficiency and safety, along with integrated marine services that support logistics and operations. The company utilizes advanced technologies, such as data analytics, to optimize routes and enhance operational efficiency.

With a strong customer base that includes global clients, Union Maritime emphasizes tailored solutions to meet complex maritime challenges. The company has a reported turnover exceeding £54 million and employs over 250 people, reflecting its significant operational scale and commitment to customer service excellence.

Westmill, established in 1988, is a prominent UK-based specialist food company and a subsidiary of Associated British Foods plc. The company manufactures and supplies a diverse range of authentic ethnic food products, including flour, rice, spices, sauces, edible oils, and noodles. Westmill primarily serves the ethnic wholesaler market, food service, industrial sectors, and grocery multiples across the UK and Europe, with a strong focus on Indian, Chinese, Thai, and Afro-Caribbean cuisines.

The product portfolio features well-known brands such as Lucky Boat noodles, Rajah spices, Habib and Tolly Boy rice, and Elephant Atta flour. Westmill has expanded its market reach through strategic acquisitions, enhancing its offerings and solidifying its position as one of Europe’s largest suppliers of ethnic food products. Headquartered in London with operational facilities in Merseyside, the company employs over 250 people and is committed to supporting ethnic communities and promoting healthy eating initiatives.

World Animal Protection is an international non-profit organization dedicated to ending animal cruelty and suffering worldwide. Founded in 1981, it operates globally with regional hubs in Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, and North America, and has offices in 14 countries, with its headquarters in London. The organization aims to create a better world for animals by protecting them from exploitation and cruelty, focusing on both wild and farmed animals.

The organization engages in various activities, including global campaigning and advocacy to improve animal welfare standards, disaster response to rescue animals affected by natural disasters, and corporate engagement to promote humane farming practices. They mobilize supporters worldwide to raise awareness about animal welfare issues and encourage public participation in their campaigns. World Animal Protection is funded through donations and grants, and it provides educational resources and opportunities for individuals to support their mission.

Adaptive specializes in custom trading technology solutions for capital markets. The company focuses on creating bespoke front-office platforms that provide competitive advantages through deep market expertise and advanced technology. Their core services include assisting clients in migrating trading technology to cloud environments, delivering tailored capital markets solutions, modernizing architecture to enhance trading agility, and providing consulting services to navigate complex challenges.

Alleyn's School is a co-educational independent school in South London, founded in 1619 by Edward Alleyn. It provides education for students aged 4 to 18 and is recognized for its strong academic tradition and broad curriculum. The school offers a variety of GCSE and A-Level subjects, promoting intellectual ambition, curiosity, and creativity among its students.

Bridewell is an independent cyber security services company founded in 2013 and headquartered in the UK, with offices in multiple locations including Cardiff, Cheltenham, Edinburgh, Houston, London, Manchester, and Reading. It has rapidly grown to become one of the largest cyber security providers in the UK and has expanded into the US to support global clients.

Cambridge Design Partnership (CDP) is an independent, employee-owned innovation and product design company based in Cambridge, UK, with an additional office in Raleigh, North Carolina. Founded in 1996, CDP specializes in end-to-end product development, collaborating with global brands and start-ups to create innovative products and services across consumer, healthcare, and industrial sectors.

CJ Lang & Son Limited is a family-owned company based in Dundee, Scotland, founded in 1919. It is the largest independent retailing and distribution business in Scotland and has been the official SPAR wholesaler for the country since 1993. The company operates over 300 SPAR convenience stores and its own chain of 82 CJ Lang stores, covering more than 45% of Scotland's independent retail market.

Cracknell is a prominent independent landscape architecture, urban design, and masterplanning practice based in Dubai, UAE, with a registered office in London, UK. Founded in 1987 by Paul Cracknell, the firm has expanded to include a global team of over 350 professionals from 35 nationalities, making it one of the largest firms in its field.

The Design Museum is a prominent UK institution focused on contemporary design, founded in 1989 by Sir Terence Conran. It serves as a vibrant space for the design industry, education, and the public to explore how design can enhance lives and contribute to a sustainable future. The museum is a registered charity that hosts innovative exhibitions, partnerships, and learning programs.

Diversity Travel is an independent travel management company founded in 2007, based in Manchester, UK, with additional offices in London, Amsterdam, Arlington (VA, USA), and Melbourne. The company specializes in meeting the unique travel needs of the charity, academic, faith, humanitarian, and not-for-profit sectors globally.

Dscout is a human-centered UX research software company that offers a flexible experience research platform. This platform is designed to assist brands and product teams in conducting user experience research that is both smarter and more empathetic. Dscout supports the entire research lifecycle, from participant recruitment to sharing actionable insights that inform strategy and product development.

edyn is a hospitality company that specializes in business travel accommodation and serviced apartments, operating since 1997. With a global supply chain of over 80,000 apartments in 260 locations, primarily across Europe, edyn features three main consumer brands: Locke, SACO, and Cove, along with additional brands like Wittenberg and The Moorgate.

EIKON Group is a global leader in digital content mastering, localization, and distribution for the entertainment industry. Founded in 2013 and headquartered in London, the company operates from offices in London, Sydney, and Los Angeles. EIKON Group features state-of-the-art facilities, including a 20,000 ft² complex in Burbank, California, equipped with advanced Dolby Atmos screening rooms for high-fidelity audio mastering.

Elexon is a UK-based not-for-profit organization that plays a vital role in the operation of the wholesale electricity market, the capacity market, and the flexibility market. With over 200 employees, Elexon administers the Balancing and Settlement Code (BSC), which governs the rules for balancing electricity supply and demand and managing financial settlements in England and Wales. The company ensures accurate market operation by comparing forecasted electricity volumes with actual consumption and production.

Aliases: Energia Group Holdings Limited, Energia Group Holdings
Description:
Energia Group is a prominent integrated energy company based in Dublin, operating throughout the island of Ireland. Founded in 1999, it focuses on renewable and flexible energy solutions, supplying about 17% of the island's electricity needs and contributing to 20% of the installed wind power capacity. The company employs around 1,100 people and generates significant revenue, with recent figures around $3.6 billion.

Energia Group operates through three main business units: Renewables, Flexible Generation, and Customer Solutions. The Renewables unit manages 358 MW of wind assets and is involved in developing additional renewable projects, including a green hydrogen facility. The Flexible Generation unit oversees 747 MW of conventional generation assets and is enhancing grid reliability with battery storage projects. The Customer Solutions unit supplies electricity and gas to over 310,000 customer sites in the Republic of Ireland and more than 550,000 in Northern Ireland, serving a diverse range of sectors including education, retail, and telecommunications. The company is committed to innovation and sustainability, aiming to lead the energy transition in Ireland.

The Energy Institute (EI) is a global professional membership body focused on the energy sector. Established in 2003, it aims to accelerate the transition to net zero carbon emissions. With around 20,000 individual members and 200 companies, the EI provides leadership, knowledge, and professional standards across the industry.

The EI offers a range of services, including professional membership and development, training courses, and events designed to enhance skills in the energy field. It also serves as a custodian of technical knowledge, providing resources to ensure high standards in health, safety, and sustainability. The institute publishes influential reports and data, such as the annual Statistical Review of World Energy, which offers comprehensive insights into global energy markets. Additionally, the EI supports collaborative initiatives that promote safety and innovation within the sector, catering to a diverse audience of energy professionals, companies, policymakers, and students.

FCMS (NW) Ltd is a social enterprise that provides health and wellbeing services, founded in 1994 as a GP cooperative in Blackpool. The organization has expanded its reach to serve areas including Fylde Coast, Lancashire & South Cumbria, South Yorkshire, Greater Manchester, and the Midlands. Operating as a private company limited by guarantee, FCMS is dedicated to delivering patient-centered care with a focus on dignity, respect, and high standards.

The company offers a variety of services, including urgent care, primary care support, call handling, diagnostics, and dental healthcare. It also provides targeted care for vulnerable communities and utilizes innovative models like virtual care to enhance accessibility. FCMS is committed to using the latest technology to improve healthcare delivery and supports its staff to ensure outstanding patient care. With a strong emphasis on community service, FCMS serves a diverse range of patients across multiple regions in the UK.

Firstup is a global enterprise platform based in San Francisco, focusing on intelligent employee communications, engagement, and experience. The company serves over 40% of Fortune 100 companies, helping them connect with their workforce through personalized, data-driven communication campaigns across various channels, including email, mobile, intranet, and digital signage.

The core offering is the Firstup Intelligent Communication Platform, which allows organizations to design and deliver engaging content tailored to every worker, particularly the deskless workforce. Key features include personalized omnichannel communication, automated employee journeys for onboarding and training, real-time engagement analytics, and AI-powered enhancements for creating hyper-personalized content. Firstup aims to reduce employee turnover, lower onboarding costs, and boost productivity by delivering relevant information effectively. The company has a diverse customer base, including JetBlue and Boston Children’s Hospital, and has achieved significant growth, surpassing $100 million in revenue in 2023.

Gerald Group is a global, independent metals trading company founded in 1962 by Gerald Lennard in Connecticut, USA. Originally starting as a copper trading business, it has grown to become the world's largest independent metals merchant. The company specializes in sourcing, trading, financing, and logistics for non-ferrous, ferrous, and precious metals, operating in over 40 countries with annual revenues exceeding $10 billion.

Headquartered in London, UK, Gerald Group has major trading hubs in Stamford, Shanghai, Geneva, Dubai, and Morges. It employs over 225 people and has maintained profitability for over 57 years. The company’s core activities include base metals merchanting, derivatives trading, and structured trade finance, with significant investments in mining operations. Gerald Group serves a diverse customer base, including miners, processors, and financial institutions, playing a vital role in the global metals supply chain.

Goodman Masson is a specialist recruitment firm founded in 1993 in central London. With a focus on the Technology and Financial Services sectors, the company has expanded into a global business with over 170 professionals and annual revenues nearing $70 million. They operate from offices in London and Düsseldorf, serving clients across various industries, including Professional Services, Banking & Financial Services, Public Sector & Not for Profit, and Commerce & Industry.

The firm specializes in recruitment for both permanent and contract roles, particularly in areas such as Software Engineering, Data, Product, Security, Cloud, and Infrastructure. Goodman Masson offers personalized recruitment solutions, talent sourcing strategies, and advice on employee engagement and diversity. They are known for their bespoke management information and reporting, as well as their support in enhancing client brand perception in the candidate marketplace. With over 30 years of experience, Goodman Masson has built a strong reputation for partnering with a diverse range of clients, from startups to large enterprises.

Inigo is a global specialist in high-risk, high-capacity insurance and reinsurance, primarily operating within the Lloyd's market. Established in 2021, the company raised $800 million from global investors and acquired assets from StarStone Underwriting, including Lloyd's Syndicate 1301. Inigo focuses on providing tailored insurance solutions for high-risk sectors, with a particular emphasis on aviation war risk and complex exposures.

The company utilizes advanced technology and data analytics to enhance risk management and support clients during loss events. Through partnerships like Insurwave, Inigo offers real-time exposure monitoring, live asset tracking, and interactive risk maps, which improve underwriting precision and facilitate timely claims handling. Inigo's leadership team comprises experienced professionals from specialty insurers, fostering a culture that values professionalism and collaboration. The company is registered in London, UK, as INIGO LIMITED and aims to deliver innovative, client-focused services in the non-life insurance sector.

Aimbridge Hospitality EMEA is the Europe, Middle East, and Africa division of Aimbridge Hospitality, a global leader in third-party hotel management with over 50 years of experience. The division manages a diverse portfolio of 114 properties across the UK, Ireland, and Europe, representing major hotel brands such as Marriott, Hilton, IHG, and Accor, as well as independent hotels.

The company offers comprehensive hotel management services, including operational management, revenue optimization, project management, and interior design. Their expertise covers various lodging segments, ensuring tailored solutions for full-service, select-service, extended stay, and lifestyle hotels. Aimbridge Hospitality EMEA focuses on delivering quality guest service and strong financial performance for each property, supported by a people-focused approach. With offices in Birmingham, Glasgow, and Amsterdam, the company provides localized management services to hotel owners, developers, and investors in the region.

Laser Clinics United Kingdom is a prominent aesthetic clinic company that specializes in medically backed cosmetic and skin treatments. The company aims to help individuals feel confident both inside and out. Their services include medical-grade laser hair removal, skin treatments, cosmetic injectables, and body sculpting procedures. The laser hair removal technology is suitable for all skin types, tones, and genders, offering fast, safe, and permanent hair reduction. Skin treatments are customized by trained therapists to meet individual concerns, while cosmetic injectables focus on anti-aging and facial rejuvenation, administered by registered professionals. 

Additionally, Laser Clinics UK offers BodySkulpt powered by TESLAFormer™, designed to help clients achieve their personalized body goals. With multiple clinic locations across the United Kingdom, the company emphasizes customized treatment plans and medically supervised care to ensure effective results. Founded in 2017 and based in London, Laser Clinics UK is committed to ethical practices and client satisfaction, positioning itself as a leader in the aesthetic clinic sector.

Maven Securities is a global proprietary trading firm founded in 2011, specializing in discretionary, systematic, and market-making trading strategies. The firm operates across various asset classes, including equities, derivatives, fixed income, commodities, and cryptocurrencies. With its headquarters in London, Maven has a significant international presence, with offices in major financial centers such as New York, Hong Kong, and Singapore, allowing for 24/7 trading coverage.

The company is 100% employee-owned and employs around 440 people from 43 nationalities, fostering a diverse and collaborative culture. Maven emphasizes core values like integrity, accountability, and creativity, encouraging employees to have a direct impact in their roles. The firm invests heavily in technology and quantitative research, utilizing advanced programming languages and proprietary systems to enhance its trading and risk management capabilities. Additionally, Maven is committed to corporate social responsibility, supporting initiatives that address inequality and promote sustainability in its communities.

McBains is a London-based property and construction consultancy with a history dating back to 1785. Originally founded by Henry Cooper, who pioneered Quantity Surveying, the company was formed in its current structure in 1993 through a merger and has evolved significantly since then. Acquired by the German investment firm RSBG in 2017, McBains now operates as a corporate entity focused on delivering tailored services to corporate clients and public bodies across the UK, Europe, and globally.

The company offers a wide range of services, including project monitoring, project management, quantity surveying, architecture, urban design, engineering, building surveying, and sustainability consulting. McBains works on projects of various scales, from minor works to large contracts exceeding £200 million, across sectors such as office, residential, education, retail, health, and infrastructure. With a client-focused approach, McBains emphasizes collaboration and innovation to provide effective solutions that meet the unique needs of its clients.

Wealthtime is a UK-based investment platform group that provides wealth management services exclusively through financial advisers. Founded in 2008, the company manages approximately £11 billion in investments for over 77,000 clients. Wealthtime operates two distinct platforms: Wealthtime and Wealthtime Classic, focusing on advanced technology and exceptional customer service to enhance the investment experience for advisers and their clients.

The company offers a wide range of investment products, including Flexible ISAs, Junior ISAs, Self-Invested Personal Pensions (SIPPs), Junior SIPPs, General Investment Accounts (GIAs), and Offshore Bonds. Wealthtime supports flexible adviser charging across all product wrappers, allowing for tailored portfolio construction. With a commitment to continuous improvement, Wealthtime collaborates with global technology leaders to ensure its platform evolves with regulatory and technological advancements, providing secure and accessible online accounts for both clients and advisers.

Aliases: One Call Insurance Services Limited, One Call Insurance Services
Description:
One Call Insurance Services Limited is a UK-based insurance broker founded in 1995 and incorporated in 2003, with its headquarters in Doncaster, South Yorkshire. The company employs over 250 staff and has a turnover exceeding £54 million. It specializes in a wide range of insurance products, including car, home, van, motorbike, travel, breakdown, and business insurance, as well as short-term insurance options.

The company offers a unique online self-service product called "One Click Insurance," allowing customers to compare quotes from various trusted insurance providers. One Call Insurance is dedicated to customer service, supported by a UK-based team of trained agents. Their commitment to excellence is recognized through multiple industry awards, including "Best Overall Insurance Broker" at the 2023 Insurance Choice Awards.

In addition to its insurance services, One Call Insurance provides mortgage and life insurance advice through its One Call Mortgage Hub. The company also actively supports local charities in Doncaster, reflecting its commitment to community engagement alongside its business operations.

Pure Data Centres Group (Pure DC) is a global developer and operator of critical digital infrastructure, specializing in hyperscale data centres. Founded in 2013 and headquartered in London, UK, the company focuses on large-scale projects in high-demand markets across Europe, the Middle East, and Asia. Pure DC manages the entire lifecycle of data centre projects, ensuring they meet the rigorous standards of major technology firms.

The company emphasizes sustainability in its operations, investing in carbon removal projects and engaging with local communities to create employment opportunities. With over 500MW of data centre capacity either live or under development, Pure DC is committed to delivering secure, resilient, and scalable digital infrastructure. Backed by Oaktree Capital Management, Pure DC is well-positioned to expand its operations and meet the growing demand for low-latency connectivity.

Richer Sounds is a British home entertainment retailer founded in 1978 by Julian Richer. The company operates 51 stores across England and offers a comprehensive online platform, telesales, and business-to-business services. It specializes in audio and video equipment, including hi-fi systems, home cinema products, and related technology.

Richer Sounds is notable for its employee ownership model, with 60% of shares held by an employee ownership trust. This initiative reflects the company's commitment to its staff, including significant bonus payouts and a progressive workplace culture. The company emphasizes expert customer service, with knowledgeable staff providing personalized assistance through various channels, including in-store visits, phone, and online interactions.

The product range includes hi-fi separates, home cinema systems, speakers, and headphones, catering to both individual consumers and business clients. Richer Sounds is recognized for its quality, expert knowledge, and dedication to customer care, making it a leading retailer in the UK home entertainment market.

Aliases: Sports Loyalty Card Limited, Sports Loyalty Card
Description:
Reward is a UK-based company founded in 2001 that focuses on enhancing customer engagement and commerce media through its cloud-based API platform. Operating in 15 countries across Europe, the Middle East, and Asia, Reward serves major financial institutions and global retailers by integrating content, advertising, and commerce to improve consumer experiences.

The company offers a range of services, including a loyalty technology platform that powers loyalty programs for leading banks, and a commerce media platform that combines purchase insights with loyalty technology. Reward also provides access to personalized offers through its Offer Cloud and utilizes data analytics to create unique marketing solutions. With a commitment to security and compliance, Reward is PCI Level 1 certified and adheres to high standards set by KPMG.

Reward aims to make everyday spending more rewarding, having given back over $1 billion in cashback rewards globally. The company has received multiple awards for its innovative solutions and is recognized as a leader in the loyalty and commerce media technology space.

Thatcham Research is a not-for-profit automotive risk intelligence organization based in the UK, established in 1969 by the motor vehicle insurance industry. Headquartered in Thatcham, Berkshire, it operates as a private limited company by guarantee without share capital. The organization specializes in automotive safety, security, and sustainable repair, providing data-driven intelligence through rigorous research and analysis.

Thatcham Research focuses on understanding and managing risk within the automotive sector. It serves a diverse range of stakeholders, including insurers, vehicle manufacturers, government bodies, technology companies, and the repair industry. The organization evaluates vehicle safety technologies, security systems, and repair methodologies, helping to improve vehicle repairability and safety outcomes. By developing standards and best practices, Thatcham Research influences industry regulations and insurance policies, ultimately supporting safer and more economically sustainable vehicle ownership and repair.

Aliases: Triple Point Investment Management LLP, Triple Point Investment Management
Description:
Triple Point LLP is an investment management firm based in London, established in 2004. The company manages over £3 billion in private, institutional, and public capital through five key investment strategies: Social Housing, Energy, Digital Infrastructure, Private Credit, and Venture Capital. Triple Point emphasizes generating positive social and environmental impact alongside financial returns, reflecting its mission where people, purpose, and profit intersect.

As a certified B Corporation and a signatory to the Principles for Responsible Investment, Triple Point is dedicated to responsible and sustainable investing. The firm employs over 220 people and fosters a culture of innovation and problem-solving. It has been recognized as one of the best places to work, highlighting its commitment to employee well-being and corporate responsibility. Triple Point serves a diverse clientele, including private investors, institutional investors, and public sector entities, focusing on both financial returns and social impact.

n2y specializes in developing special education curriculum and materials to support educators, speech pathologists, and professionals working with children with special needs. Founded in 1997 by speech-language pathologist Jacquie Clark, the company initially created *News-2-You*, a weekly newspaper designed to enhance communication and social skills for special education students.

The company offers a suite of integrated digital solutions tailored for special education from Pre-K through transition age. Key products include the Unique Learning System (ULS), which aligns instruction with state standards; *News-2-You*, which provides accessible news content; SymbolStix, a symbol-based language system; Positivity, a behavioral management tool; and Polaris, a platform for IEP development and management. These solutions aim to improve student performance, teacher efficiency, and overall satisfaction among parents and educators.

In March 2024, n2y merged with Texthelp to expand their impact globally, focusing on promoting accessibility and inclusivity for neurodiverse learners. With over 25 years of experience, n2y serves millions of students and thousands of educators across the United States.

Visualsoft is a UK-based full-service eCommerce agency with over 25 years of experience, specializing in website development and digital marketing strategies. Founded in 1998 and headquartered in Stockton On Tees, the company serves some of the UK’s largest brands, providing innovative and performance-driven eCommerce solutions.

The agency offers a variety of services, including custom eCommerce website design and development, digital marketing strategies like PPC management and affiliate marketing, and ongoing website management. Visualsoft also develops proprietary technology, such as the VS Commerce platform, and partners with leading platforms like Shopify Plus and Klaviyo to enhance online retail experiences.

With a team of 201-500 employees, Visualsoft operates from offices in Teesside, Newcastle, and Manchester. The company has successfully collaborated with clients across various industries, achieving significant revenue growth and improved customer engagement through tailored digital solutions.

4most (Europe) Limited is a data analytics and credit risk consultancy based in London, UK, with additional offices in Leeds, Amsterdam, Dubai, Edinburgh, and Bangalore, India. Founded in 2011, it has become the UK's largest specialist analytics consultancy, serving clients primarily in the financial services sector.

Accurx is a British software company founded in May 2016, dedicated to improving communication within the healthcare sector, particularly for the NHS in England. Initially focused on tackling antimicrobial resistance, the company shifted its focus in 2018 to address broader communication challenges faced by healthcare professionals and patients.

Alternative Heat Ltd is a prominent company focused on renewable heating and commercial energy solutions, operating in the UK, Ireland, and mainland Europe since its founding in 2003. The company specializes in the design, supply, installation, and commissioning of sustainable heating systems, including biomass boilers, ground and air source heat pumps, solar panels, and underfloor heating systems. They also offer offsite prefabrication of Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing (MEP) systems, ensuring efficient project delivery for various sectors.

Bede Gaming Limited is a private company that develops and provides online gambling platforms and software. Founded in 2011 and headquartered in Newcastle upon Tyne, England, the company has additional offices in London, Sofia, and Toronto. Bede Gaming serves a global market, offering solutions to online casinos, bingo operators, lotteries, and sports betting companies in regulated markets.

CPW is a UK-based environmental building services consultancy that specializes in mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) engineering. Founded in 1978, the company focuses on sustainable design and Zero-Carbon innovation, delivering engineering solutions that minimize environmental impact. CPW emphasizes energy-efficient practices to create buildings that support better lives for future generations.

Codasip is a European company that specializes in custom processor solutions based on the open RISC-V instruction set architecture (ISA). Founded in 2014 by Dr. Karel Masařík, Codasip has its roots in extensive research at Brno University of Technology. The company is recognized as a leader in the RISC-V sector, serving clients in over 15 countries and contributing to billions of chips worldwide.

DatacenterDynamics (DCD) is a global media and events company focused on the data center and digital infrastructure industry. Founded in 2009 and based in London, DCD provides valuable information, insights, and networking opportunities for professionals involved in the planning, building, and operation of digital infrastructure.

Dream Games is a mobile gaming company based in Istanbul, Turkey, founded in 2019 by five former executives from Peak Games, including CEO Soner Aydemir. The company focuses on developing high-quality puzzle games that prioritize craftsmanship and player-centric design.

Enotria&Coe is a prominent UK-based distributor of premium wines and spirits, established in 2015 through the merger of two historic companies: Enotria and Coe Vintners. With roots dating back to 1972 and 1930 respectively, the company combines decades of expertise in the distribution of exceptional alcoholic beverages.

The extensive portfolio includes a wide variety of wines—red, white, sparkling, dessert, fortified, and rosé—as well as a diverse selection of spirits such as rum, vodka, liqueurs, brandy, whiskey, Scotch whisky, tequila, and mezcal. Enotria&Coe represents over 100 exclusive producers worldwide, featuring both iconic and artisan brands. The company primarily serves premium bars, restaurants, and specialist retailers across the UK, utilizing a hybrid partnership model that enhances its distribution capabilities.

Enotria&Coe is dedicated to sustainability and industry leadership, being a founding member of the Sustainable Wine Roundtable. The company also focuses on community and education by offering tailored training programs to empower customer teams in serving and selling their products effectively.

Flawless is a London-based technology company that specializes in artificial intelligence solutions for the film and television industry. Founded in 2019 by industry veterans, including director Scott Mann and CEO Nick Lynes, Flawless focuses on enhancing global storytelling through innovative visual and performance editing tools. The company aims to empower creators by overcoming traditional barriers such as language and budget constraints, making filmmaking more affordable and accessible.

Flawless offers several advanced products, including TrueSync, which visually translates foreign language films by synchronizing lip movements and facial expressions with dubbed audio. Their DeepEditor tool assists filmmakers in refining performances and visual elements without costly reshoots. Additionally, Flawless provides AI-assisted performance editing and generative AI tools for automated dialogue replacement and CGI generation. The company primarily serves filmmakers, content owners, and entertainment companies looking to expand their global reach while maintaining quality and authenticity.

Gordon Murray Group (GMG) is a British advanced engineering and design company based in Surrey, specializing in high-performance supercars and innovative automotive technologies. Founded by renowned designer Gordon Murray, the company emphasizes lightweight construction and advanced design to create exceptional driving experiences. GMG operates globally, with service and support centers in various countries, ensuring premium customer service for vehicle owners.

The Group consists of two main divisions: Gordon Murray Advanced Engineering (GMAe) and Gordon Murray Automotive (GMA). GMAe focuses on design and innovation, contributing to the development of future mobility platforms and lightweight structures. GMA, established in 2017, manufactures limited-run, hand-built supercars, including the T.50 and T.33 models, which are known for their precision engineering and driver engagement.

In addition to its automotive focus, GMG includes Gordon Murray Technologies (GMT), which develops electric and autonomous vehicle technologies. The Group's commitment to innovation and engineering excellence reflects Gordon Murray's legacy in the automotive industry.

Gresham Technologies is a British software company that specializes in real-time transaction control and enterprise data integrity solutions for the financial services industry. Founded in 1969 and headquartered in London, the company is publicly listed on the London Stock Exchange. Gresham has a global presence with offices in Europe, North America, and Asia Pacific, serving financial institutions worldwide.

The company focuses on enterprise data automation, helping clients simplify complex data operations. Gresham offers a suite of solutions that includes data management, reconciliation, and regulatory reporting. Its flagship product, the Clareti platform, automates compliance and operational processes, while Control Cloud provides automated reconciliation technology tailored for financial services. Gresham's clients include banks and investment firms, and its strategic acquisitions have enhanced its capabilities in the financial services sector. With a revenue of approximately $62.1 million and a dedicated team of around 241 employees, Gresham Technologies is a key player in the RegTech and financial data management markets.

Gumtree.com is a leading online classified advertisement and community website based in the UK, founded in 2000 and headquartered in London. It connects people across the country to buy, sell, find, or swap a variety of goods and services within their local neighborhoods. The platform is recognized as the UK's number one classifieds site, attracting significantly higher traffic than its competitors.

Gumtree offers a user-friendly interface for posting free or paid advertisements across various categories, including jobs, property, cars, and general items. It serves both private individuals and businesses, facilitating local transactions and community connections. With an annual turnover exceeding £54 million, Gumtree supports millions of users each month, making it a vital resource for local classifieds and community engagement in the UK.

Crownpeak is a global leader in digital experience management, established in 2001. The company focuses on providing cloud-native Digital Experience Platform (DXP) solutions that help businesses enhance their digital presence. Crownpeak's offerings include the FirstSpirit CMS, which simplifies content management, and Fredhopper, an AI-driven product discovery engine that personalizes eCommerce experiences. They also provide digital accessibility solutions to ensure compliance with accessibility standards.

With over 600 customers worldwide, including prominent brands in America, EMEA, and Australia, Crownpeak supports its clients through a team of more than 300 experts. The company emphasizes strategic SaaS management to improve operational efficiency and cost control, partnering with Spendflo to enhance its procurement and compliance processes. Crownpeak is dedicated to helping enterprises scale their digital growth and deliver high-quality, accessible digital experiences.

Makers is a UK-based tech education and talent organization founded in 2013 by Evgeny Shadchnev and Rob Johnson. As Europe’s first coding bootcamp, it has grown into a comprehensive service aimed at equipping individuals and businesses with essential skills for the AI-driven economy. Makers is a certified B Corp, demonstrating its commitment to high standards of performance, accountability, and sustainable practices.

The company offers a variety of tech education programs, including software development, DevOps and cloud engineering, data engineering, test engineering, and data analytics. These programs are delivered through immersive bootcamp-style training, typically lasting 16 weeks, and focus on both technical skills and problem-solving abilities. Makers also provides apprenticeships and tailored upskilling programs for businesses, helping to build AI-confident tech teams.

Makers has trained over 3,000 individuals and partnered with more than 300 organizations, including notable companies like Deliveroo, British Gas, and Tesco. The company is dedicated to promoting diversity and inclusivity in tech, collaborating with various community organizations to support underrepresented groups in the industry.

Mako Trading is a proprietary trading firm established in 1999, specializing in providing liquidity to global derivatives markets. The company operates across major exchanges in Europe, Asia Pacific, and the Americas, focusing on options market making. Mako combines quantitative research, advanced technology, and trader expertise to navigate complex and fast-moving markets effectively.

The firm offers market-making services in exchange-traded fixed income, equities, and commodities. Its trading approach utilizes high-frequency methodologies and proprietary strategies, supported by a technology platform that enables rapid trading in highly liquid products. Mako's quantitative research team develops data-informed trading strategies using advanced algorithms and volatility pricing models, enhancing precision in option valuation. The firm is headquartered in London and operates as Mako Trading Holdings Limited.

MLL Legal is a full-service Swiss law firm with offices in Zurich, Geneva, Zug, Lausanne, London, and Madrid. The firm specializes in a wide range of commercial law areas, including intellectual property, corporate and M&A, banking and finance, employment law, and competition law. MLL Legal is recognized for its strong international presence and pragmatic, business-oriented legal advice.

The firm has a notable focus on intellectual property, providing services such as trademark registration, enforcement, and litigation. In corporate and M&A, MLL Legal supports national and cross-border transactions, offering insights into corporate governance and commercial contracts. Their banking and finance practice covers various aspects, including asset management and regulatory compliance. Additionally, MLL Legal advises on competition law, employment matters, and capital markets, serving a diverse clientele that includes individuals, corporations, and private equity funds.

With a multilingual team, MLL Legal effectively supports clients across different jurisdictions, combining legal expertise with practical business insight.

MMT Digital is a UK-based digital agency founded in 1999, specializing in creating bespoke websites and digital applications. With a focus on user experience and business growth, MMT Digital operates as the technology enablement division of MSQ Partners, boasting offices in London, Uppingham, Leeds, and teams in India and the US. The agency employs over 65 people and generates around $68 million in revenue.

The company offers three main service areas: creating meaningful digital experiences, developing innovative products, and facilitating accelerated transformation for enterprises. MMT Digital emphasizes a collaborative and agile approach, helping clients enhance their digital capabilities and achieve significant business results. Their portfolio includes award-winning projects, such as Vodafone’s Digital X Programme, which features the first end-to-end chatbot-driven sales journey in telecoms. MMT Digital partners with ambitious brands across various sectors, focusing on delivering tailored digital solutions that drive measurable growth.

Monex Europe is a UK-based foreign exchange company that specializes in currency exchange and international payment services for corporate and institutional clients. As an independent subsidiary of Monex Europe Holdings Limited, it is part of the global financial services group Monex S.A.P.I. de C.V. The company offers a variety of foreign exchange solutions, including spot and forward FX contracts, market orders, margin credit facilities, and customized FX hedging solutions.

In addition to its FX services, Monex Europe provides global payment and technology solutions that enable clients to send and receive funds in multiple currencies. The company supports operational efficiency through API integration, allowing clients to outsource their global payment processes. Monex Europe's diverse client base includes multinational corporations, investment funds, financial institutions, and small to medium-sized enterprises across various sectors. Headquartered in London, Monex Europe is regulated by the UK Financial Conduct Authority, ensuring compliance with high regulatory standards.

Mount Anvil is a British residential property developer based in London, founded in 1991 by Killian Hurley. The company specializes in creating high-quality residential developments that foster thriving communities. With over 30 years of experience, Mount Anvil has earned a strong reputation for quality and sustainability, winning multiple industry awards, including Developer of the Year.

The company focuses on large-scale residential projects, emphasizing community benefit and environmental sustainability. Notable developments include the Vauxhall scheme, which features Britain’s tallest residential brick tower, and the Wyvil Primary School, enhancing local infrastructure. Mount Anvil collaborates with housing associations, local authorities, and other stakeholders to deliver these projects, ensuring they meet the needs of homebuyers in London. Their commitment to sustainable practices includes partnerships with ecology experts to promote biodiversity in their developments.

myenergi is a British renewable energy technology company founded in 2016, headquartered in Stallingborough, Lincolnshire. The company focuses on designing, manufacturing, and installing smart energy products that help homeowners maximize renewable energy use, reduce their carbon footprint, and achieve greater energy independence.

The product lineup includes the zappi, a smart electric vehicle charger that utilizes excess solar energy, and the eddi, a power diverter that redirects surplus energy to heat water and other systems. Additionally, the hub serves as a central control unit for managing all myenergi products from one interface. The company also offers installation services through partnerships with various installers.

myenergi targets eco-conscious homeowners, particularly those with solar PV systems and electric vehicles. With a commitment to sustainability, the company operates its headquarters using 100% renewable electricity and sells excess solar energy back to the grid. This approach supports its mission to create an accessible eco-smart home ecosystem and promote a greener future.

Nine United is an international family of companies with a diverse portfolio that includes design, retail branding, supply and trading, property management, and philanthropic activities. Founded in 1975 in Ringkøbing, Denmark, the company has grown to have a global presence, emphasizing quality and tailored solutions.

The company specializes in designing and producing high-quality furniture, lighting, and accessories, with over 20 years of experience in delivering products worldwide. Through its brand STUDIO 9, Nine United offers customized retail branding concepts, enhancing shopping experiences for clients. Its supply and trading operations manage sourcing for a wide range of products, including household appliances and consumer goods, and expanded to include personal protective equipment during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Nine United also owns and manages properties in major European cities like Copenhagen, Paris, and London, contributing to its diversified business model. Additionally, the company operates non-profit foundations that support various organizations and projects, focusing on corporate social responsibility and sustainable development initiatives.

Orgvue is a UK-based private limited company that specializes in data-driven Software as a Service (SaaS) solutions for organizational design, workforce analysis, and operational modeling. Founded in 2008 and headquartered in London, Orgvue integrates organizational data from various systems to help enterprises visualize, model, and optimize their business operations in real time.

The company offers a strategic human capital software platform that includes tools for workforce analysis and modeling, real-time data visualization, and operational modeling capabilities. This platform supports business, finance, and HR leaders in analyzing and re-engineering operations for improved efficiency. Orgvue serves a diverse clientele, including over 25 companies listed on the FTSE100 and S&P100 indices, as well as leading global management consultancies. With a strong focus on data-driven decision-making, Orgvue aims to enhance strategic human capital management for large enterprises worldwide.

Company Name: PGMBM Law Ltd t/a Pogust Goodhead
Aliases: PGMBM Law Ltd t/a Pogust Goodhead
Description:
Pogust Goodhead is an international law firm based in London, specializing in class-action and group litigation. Founded in 2018 by Harris Pogust and Tom Goodhead, the firm has grown to include over 100 lawyers and more than 500 staff members. It focuses on environmental law, consumer protection, and human rights, helping individuals, groups, and businesses achieve justice.

The firm is known for its work on large-scale litigation against multinational corporations, particularly in cases involving environmental disasters and consumer rights violations. A notable achievement includes securing a landmark judgment for over 620,000 victims of the 2015 Mariana dam disaster in Brazil, allowing them to seek redress against BHP in the courts of England and Wales. Pogust Goodhead also leads significant consumer protection cases, such as the Volkswagen diesel emissions fraud litigation and the British Airways data breach claim.

With offices in London and Edinburgh, and a strong presence in the United States, Pogust Goodhead is committed to advocating for victims of corporate wrongdoing and human rights abuses. The firm emphasizes environmental, social, and governance principles in its legal practice.

Point Blank Music School is a renowned music education institution founded in London in 1994 by musician and producer Robert Cowan. It specializes in music production, sound engineering, DJing, singing, songwriting, radio broadcasting, and music business. With campuses in London, Los Angeles, Ibiza, and Mumbai, as well as online courses, it provides accessible education to students worldwide. The school has been recognized as the world's "Best Music Production & DJ School" by DJ Mag readers.

The school offers a variety of programs, including short certificates, diplomas, and full Higher Education degrees, such as a BA (Hons) in Music Production and Sound Engineering. Courses are taught by industry professionals in small class sizes, ensuring personalized instruction. Facilities include professional recording studios with industry-standard equipment and software training in tools like Ableton Live and Logic Pro. Point Blank attracts a diverse international student body and boasts a successful alumni network, highlighting its significant influence in the music industry.

Signal AI is a London-based artificial intelligence company founded in 2013 by David Benigson, Miguel Martinez, and Wesley Hall. The company specializes in reputation and risk intelligence, helping businesses make informed decisions by transforming large volumes of information into actionable insights.

Their AI-powered platform aggregates and analyzes data from various sources, including digital media, print, broadcast, and social media. This technology provides real-time insights that assist organizations in monitoring their reputation, managing supply chain risks, and staying compliant with regulatory changes. Key services include reputation intelligence, risk intelligence, compliance monitoring, and predictive analytics.

Signal AI serves over 650 customers globally, including notable corporations like Bank of America Securities, Google, and Exxon Mobil. The company has raised over $100 million in funding, with a focus on expanding its predictive analytics capabilities and advancing AI research and development. The company culture promotes innovation, diversity, and a people-centric approach.

Aliases: The Berkeley Hotel Limited, The Berkeley Hotel
Description:
The Berkeley Group Holdings plc is a prominent British property developer and house-builder based in Cobham, Surrey. Established in 1976 as Berkeley Homes, the company is publicly listed on the London Stock Exchange and is part of the FTSE 100 Index. It specializes in developing and selling a diverse range of residential and commercial properties, primarily in London, Birmingham, and the South of England.

Berkeley's portfolio includes executive homes, family houses, city apartments, student residences, and mixed-use developments. The company operates under several brands, such as Berkeley, St George, and St Edward, catering to various customer segments. Berkeley is committed to building high-quality homes and fostering local communities, with a focus on environmental sustainability and social mobility. Its long-term strategy, *Our Vision 2030*, aims to balance business growth with positive societal and environmental impacts.

Aliases: THE CHILDREN'S INVESTMENT FUND FOUNDATION (UK), THE CHILDREN'S INVESTMENT FUND FOUNDATION
Description:
The Children's Investment Fund Foundation (CIFF) is an independent philanthropic organization founded in 2002 by Sir Chris Hohn and Jamie Cooper-Hohn. Based in London, with offices in several major cities, CIFF focuses on improving the lives of children living in poverty in developing countries through strategic, data-driven philanthropy. The foundation manages a significant endowment and has disbursed over $2.1 billion in charitable grants, emphasizing measurable impact.

CIFF's core areas of focus include child health and development, climate change, sexual and reproductive health and rights, gender equality, and child protection. The foundation invests in initiatives that enhance child and maternal health, support climate action, empower adolescents, and combat child labor and exploitation. CIFF collaborates with various partners, including NGOs, governments, and international organizations, to create sustainable solutions and improve outcomes for vulnerable communities.

The Dean Close Foundation is a registered educational charity located in Cheltenham, England. It is dedicated to providing high-quality education for children from nursery age to 18 years. Established in 2015, the Foundation grew from Dean Close School, which was founded in 1886. It operates a diverse family of independent schools and nurseries, currently comprising five schools and six nurseries, all guided by a Christian ethos and core values of courage, contribution, and love.

The Foundation emphasizes academic excellence alongside character development, encouraging students to engage in their communities and express respect and compassion. It offers a broad educational experience that includes music, theatre, and sports, ensuring that each child can reach their full potential. By fostering a strong community among staff and students, the Dean Close Foundation provides a nurturing environment that balances rigorous academics with personal growth for families seeking independent, values-driven education.

Tractable is a technology company founded in 2014, based in London, UK, with additional offices in North America, Asia, and Europe. The company specializes in applying artificial intelligence, particularly deep learning and computer vision, to automate visual damage assessments in the automotive and property sectors. With a workforce of 201-500 employees, Tractable serves over 35 leading insurance and automotive companies, including more than 10 Fortune Global 500 firms.

Tractable's core offerings include AI damage appraisal, which analyzes images of damaged vehicles or properties to provide instant and accurate assessments. This technology accelerates claim settlements and repair decisions, enhancing customer experience and operational efficiency. The company also provides accident recovery solutions that automate damage evaluations and predict repair costs in real time. Additionally, Tractable offers tools for vehicle condition assessment and supports automotive recyclers in optimizing the recycling of end-of-life vehicles. Recognized as a top AI company globally, Tractable achieved unicorn status in 2021 and continues to innovate in the insurance and automotive industries.

Wanstor is a UK-based IT services and solutions provider founded in 2002. The company specializes in delivering comprehensive IT support and consultancy to help organizations improve and grow through technology. With over 200 employees and offices in London and Hemel Hempstead, Wanstor serves a diverse client base, including some of the UK's largest brands, focusing on creating scalable and secure IT environments.

Wanstor offers a wide range of services, including managed IT services, cloud solutions, data center management, data infrastructure and analytics, business voice services, and cybersecurity. The company utilizes advanced IT service management tools like the POPX MSP Platform and ServiceNow to enhance service delivery and operational efficiency. Wanstor is committed to fostering a motivated workforce through employee engagement initiatives and professional development programs, ensuring they remain a trusted partner for organizations looking to leverage technology for business success.
